message.content:
In a parallel chat, the following edit is pending.  I am wondering if there are AXLA voicings we ought to apply to this, related to the MCM doc. Thoughts?


=== Consumer Filesystem Layout

{rbtr_consumer} configuration lives under a single moorings directory.
The references below are the canonical filesystem paths; the specification cites these linked terms rather than
repeating literal paths, so a future layout change touches only the mapping section.

[[rbsld_moorings]]
{rbsld_moorings}::
The moorings directory — the single root holding all consumer-authored Recipe Bottle configuration: regime assig
nment files, per-{at_nameplate} subdirectories, and the {rbsld_vessels}.

[[rbsld_vessels]]
{rbsld_vessels}::
The vessel directory.
Holds one subdirectory per {rbtga_vessel}, each named by its {rbrv_sigil} and carrying that vessel's {rbrv_prefi
x} assignment file at {rbsld_vessels}{rbrv_sigil}/rbrv.env.

[[rbsrf_rbrr]]
{rbsrf_rbrr}::
The {rbrr_regime} assignment file.

[[rbsrf_rbrd]]
{rbsrf_rbrd}::
The {rbrd_regime} assignment file.

[[rbsrf_rbrm]]
{rbsrf_rbrm}::
The {rbrm_regime} assignment file.
The {rbrn_prefix} assignment file is per-nameplate, at {rbsld_moorings}{rbrn_moniker}/rbrn.env.

## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

380	
381	== Core Principles
382	
383	The Axial Lexicon provides reusable, atomic terminology for technical specifications.
384	Each term maintains precise semantics while adapting to implementation context.
385	Specifications selectively import only needed axial terms, avoiding vocabulary bloat.
386	
387	== Motif Vocabulary
388	
389	AXL terms are
390	{axl_motif_s}
391	- abstract patterns that concrete specifications voice in their local context.
392	
393	[[axl_motif]]
394	{axl_motif}::
395	An abstract pattern defined in the Axial Lexicon that can be voiced by concrete terms in specifications.
396	A
397	{axl_motif}
398	captures the essential shape of a concept while remaining independent of implementation context.
399	Specifications voice
400	{axl_motif_s}
401	through local terms that bind the abstract pattern to specific environments.
402	
403	[[axl_voicing]]
404	{axl_voicing}::
405	A concrete term in a specification that gives local expression to A
406	{axl_motif}.
407	Each
408	{axl_voicing}
409	adapts the abstract pattern to a specific context while preserving the
410	{axl_motif_s}
411	essential character.
412	
413	[[axl_voices]]
414	{axl_voices}::
415	The relationship where a concrete term expresses a
416	{axl_motif}
417	in a specific context.
418	When a term
419	{axl_voices}
420	a
421	{axl_motif},
422	it gives the abstract pattern local form and implementation-specific behavior.
423	
424	[[axl_evokes]]
425	{axl_evokes}::
426	The relationship where A
427	{axl_motif}
428	calls forth its
429	{axl_voicing_s}
430	in the reader's understanding.
431	When encountering A
432	{axl_motif},
433	it
434	{axl_evokes}
435	the concrete realizations known from various specifications.
436	
437	[[axl_annotation]]
438	{axl_annotation}::
439	A
440	{xref_MCM}
441	comment line that declares the
442	{axl_motif_s}
443	a term
444	{axl_voices}.
445	The
446	{axl_annotation}
447	appears between anchor and definition as a space-separated list of prefix terms on a comment line with no space after `//`.
448	+
449	Format: `//relationship primary [dimension ...]`
450	+
451	* First term: the relationship (typically `axl_voices`)
452	* Second term: the primary
453	{axl_motif}
454	being voiced
455	* Remaining terms: dimension terms (prefixed `axd_`) specifying characteristics
456	+
457	Examples:
458	+
459	* `//axl_voices axc_call` - simple voicing
460	* `//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_nullable` - voicing with dimensions
461	+
462	Category prefixes carry semantic meaning, eliminating need for punctuation.
463	Position determines role: relationship first, primary second, dimensions following.
464	This design prioritizes LLM interpretability over human visual parsing.
465	When ambiguity or friction arises during LLM processing, the format should evolve to resolve it.
466	
467	== Motif Implications
468	
469	{axl_motif_s}
470	carry implied characteristics beyond their definitions.
471	These implications guide how
472	{axl_voicing_s}
473	should behave:
474	
475	* Anchor placement expectations per
476	{xref_MCM}
477	form patterns
478	* Type/instance level - where the
479	{axl_motif}
480	sits in abstraction hierarchy
481	* Contextual requirements - parent structures or operations the
482	{axl_motif}
483	exists within
484	* Voicing expectation - whether the
485	{axl_motif}
486	typically requires local voicing
487	
488	== Form Expectations
489	
490	Each AXL category implies an expected
491	{xref_MCM}
492	definition form pattern for documents using that category.
493	During type-scheme linting, these expectations guide validation of definition structure.
494	
495	* {axc_*} control terms expect
496	{xref_MCM}
497	`mcm_form_deflist`
498	* {axj_structure} definitions expect
499	{xref_MCM}
500	`mcm_form_section`
501	* {axj_field} definitions expect
502	{xref_MCM}
503	`mcm_form_bullet` under parent structure
504	* {axk_premise} definitions expect
505	{xref_MCM}
506	`mcm_form_deflist`
507	* Most other categories expect
508	{xref_MCM}
509	`mcm_form_deflist`
510	
511	These expectations support the
512	{axl_annotation}
513	pattern where documents include `//axl_voices motif ...` markers that are validated when AXL is in context.
514	
515	== Axial Control Terms
516	
517	[[axc_variable]]
518	{axc_variable}::
519	A
520	{axl_motif}
521	representing a temporary value holder denoted by distinctive delimiters that exists for the duration of an operational sequence.
522	Implementation varies by context: shell variables in bash, dictionary entries in Python, sessionStorage in browser JavaScript.
523	
524	[[axc_fatal]]
525	{axc_fatal}::
526	A
527	{axl_motif}
528	representing immediate termination of the operational sequence with error status, preventing all subsequent steps from executing.
529	Requires operator intervention or error recovery before retry.
530	
531	[[axc_warn]]
532	{axc_warn}::
533	A
534	{axl_motif}
535	representing display of a non-blocking warning message while allowing the operational sequence to continue execution.
536	Alerts operators to concerning conditions without halting progress.
537	
538	[[axc_store]]
539	{axc_store}::
540	A
541	{axl_motif}
542	representing capture of a value from an operation result or computation into a
543	{axc_variable}
544	for later reference.
545	Context determines storage mechanism while preserving semantic intent.
546	
547	[[axc_use]]
548	{axc_use}::
549	A
550	{axl_motif}
551	representing reference to a previously stored
552	{axc_variable}
553	value in the current operation.
554	Retrieves stored values from context-appropriate storage location.
555	
556	[[axc_require]]
557	{axc_require}::
558	A
559	{axl_motif}
560	representing validation check that confirms an expected condition, triggering
561	{axc_fatal}
562	or alternate execution path if not met.
563	Enforces preconditions and invariants throughout execution.
564	
565	[[axc_poll]]
566	{axc_poll}::
567	A
568	{axl_motif}
569	representing repeated execution of a status check at defined intervals until completion condition or timeout.
570	Implements retry logic with configurable intervals and limits.
571	
572	[[axc_call]]
573	{axc_call}::
574	A
575	{axl_motif}
576	representing direct invocation of an external service, API endpoint, or command.
577	Captures both success and error responses for processing.
578	
579	[[axc_submit]]
580	{axc_submit}::
581	A
582	{axl_motif}
583	representing initiation of an asynchronous operation that returns immediately with a handle for later completion tracking.
584	Distinct from
585	{axc_call}
586	which expects synchronous response.
587	
588	[[axc_await]]
589	{axc_await}::
590	A
591	{axl_motif}
592	representing blocking wait on a submitted asynchronous operation until completion.
593	Polls operation status until done, then returns final result or error.
594	
595	[[axc_show]]
596	{axc_show}::
597	A
598	{axl_motif}
599	representing presentation of operational facts or values to the operator.
600	Implementation varies from console output to UI display based on context.
601	
602	[[axc_step]]
603	{axc_step}::
604	A
605	{axl_motif}
606	representing a normal progress message indicating successful completion of a processing stage.
607	Provides operational visibility without implying warnings or errors.
608	
609	== Axial Environment Terms
610	
611	{axe_environment_s}
612	specify execution contexts for control operations.
613	{axl_voicing_s}
614	of
615	{axc_call},
616	{axc_warn},
617	{axc_fatal}
618	and similar control motifs describe step-level behavior within operations.
619	
620	[[axe_rest_api]]
621	{axe_rest_api}::
622	A
623	{axl_motif}
624	representing invocation of an HTTP REST API endpoint.
625	Request/response via JSON or other structured format.
626	Authentication via bearer tokens, API keys, or similar.
627	Calls return HTTP status codes and response bodies.
628	
629	[[axe_console]]
630	{axe_console}::
631	A
632	{axl_motif}
633	representing direct terminal invocation.
634	Standard output and standard error are visible to the user.
635	Output can be routed directly to stdout/stderr.
636	
637	[[axe_mcp_transport]]
638	{axe_mcp_transport}::
639	A
640	{axl_motif}
641	representing invocation through the MCP (Model Context Protocol) transport layer.
642	Standard error is swallowed by the transport — all output must be captured in a return buffer to reach the caller.
643	
644	[[axe_daemon_runtime]]
645	{axe_daemon_runtime}::
646	A
647	{axl_motif}
648	representing a long-running system process.
649	Errors are logged, not displayed.
650	Fatal means graceful shutdown with cleanup.
651	No human in immediate feedback loop.
652	
653	== Axial Operation Terms
654	
655	[[axo_procedure]]
656	{axo_procedure}::
657	A
658	{axl_motif}
659	representing an executable operation with defined inputs, behavior, outputs, and completion semantics.
660	Standalone operations not affiliated with an entity.
661	Voiced at the
662	{axl_definition_site}
663	via
664	{axvo_procedure}.
665	
666	[[axo_role]]
667	{axo_role}::
668	A
669	{axl_motif}
670	representing an operational identity pattern that defines capabilities and permissions.
671	Determines available operations and access boundaries.
672	
673	[[axo_identity]]
674	{axo_identity}::
675	A
676	{axl_motif}
677	representing the abstract concept of an operational identity.
678	An entity capable of authentication and holding authorities within a system.
679	{axo_identity}
680	is the pattern;
681	{axo_actor_s}
682	are named instantiations with specific bindings.
683	
684	[[axo_actor]]
685	{axo_actor}::
686	A
687	{axl_motif}
688	representing a specific named identity with defined responsibilities and exclusive authority over an operational domain.
689	{axo_actor_s}
690	instantiate
691	{axo_identity}
692	with concrete bindings (human via OAuth, service via service account) and permissions.
693	
694	[[axo_entity]]
695	{axo_entity}::
696	A
697	{axl_motif}
698	representing an object with both structure and behavior.
699	Unlike
700	{axr_record}
701	which captures pure data,
702	{axo_entity}
703	includes methods, builder patterns, or lifecycle operations.
704	{axl_voicing_s}
705	describe the entity's data members and its behavioral interface (construction, mutation, registration).
706	Typical manifestations include Rust structs with `impl` blocks, builder-pattern APIs, or objects that participate in registration protocols.
707	
708	== Axial Type Terms
709	
710	[[axt_type]]
711	{axt_type}::
712	A
713	{axl_motif}
714	representing a data format constraint that defines valid values and structures.
715	Enforces data validation and enables type-safe operations.
716	
717	[[axt_string]]
718	{axt_string}::
719	A
720	{axl_motif}
721	representing text data type with optional pattern validation.
722	{axl_voicing_s}
723	may address context-specific concerns including:
724	+
725	* Encoding (ASCII, UTF-8, locale-dependent character sets)
726	* Termination semantics (null-terminated, length-prefixed, delimiter-bounded)
727	* Mutability (immutable values, mutable buffers)
728	* Memory management (owned, borrowed, reference-counted)
729	* Safety constraints (shell-safe, URL-safe, JSON-safe, SQL-safe)
730	* Format patterns (timestamps, identifiers, hashes, paths)
731	* Size and bounds (fixed-length, maximum length, unbounded)
732	
733	[[axt_number]]
734	{axt_number}::
735	A
736	{axl_motif}
737	representing numeric value type supporting integers and floating point.
738	May specify range constraints or precision requirements.
739	
740	[[axt_boolean]]
741	{axt_boolean}::
742	A
743	{axl_motif}
744	representing binary true/false value type.
745	Maps to language-specific boolean implementations.
746	
747	[[axt_array]]
748	{axt_array}::
749	A
750	{axl_motif}
751	representing ordered collection of typed elements.
752	Specifies element type and optional size constraints.
753	
754	[[axt_object]]
755	{axt_object}::
756	A
757	{axl_motif}
758	representing named field collection forming a composite type.
759	Contains typed fields with defined cardinality.
760	
761	[[axt_map]]
762	{axt_map}::
763	A
764	{axl_motif}
765	representing a dictionary structure with typed keys and typed values.
766	2-arity: reads two
767	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
768	from following text — the first identifies the key type, the second identifies the value type.
769	Keys are unique within the map; values are accessed by key lookup.
770	Serialization format determines key ordering guarantees and key type constraints (e.g., JSON restricts keys to strings).
771	
772	[[axt_enumeration]]
773	{axt_enumeration}::
774	A
775	{axl_motif}
776	representing constrained set of valid string values.
777	Restricts values to predefined options.
778	
779	[[axt_enum_value]]
780	{axt_enum_value}::
781	A
782	{axl_motif}
783	representing single allowed option within an
784	{axt_enumeration}.
785	Must be explicitly listed in enumeration definition.
786	+
787	Encoding is carried by an
788	{axd_dimension}
789	on the voicing line — e.g.,
790	{axd_string}
791	for string-token-encoded values.
792	The encoding
793	{axd_dimension}
794	specifies the
795	{mcm_sprue}
796	arity for the wire-level value.
797	Exactly one encoding dimension must qualify every
798	{axt_enum_value}
799	voicing whose
800	{axt_enumeration}
801	participates in a wire form.
802	
803	[[axt_insignia]]
804	{axt_insignia}::
805	A
806	{axl_motif}
807	representing a glyph-prefixed typed identity string with seed-based allocation.
808	Extends
809	{axt_string}
810	with structural properties: a unicode verification glyph (decorative in input, mandatory in output), a fixed encoding format, a monotonic seed allocation mechanism, and optional hierarchical embedding (parent identity encoded as a prefix substring).
811	+
812	Every
813	{axt_insignia}
814	declares an encoding nature via
815	{axd_dimension_s}:
816	{axd_algebraic}
817	or
818	{axd_temporal}.
819	
820	[[axt_variant]]
821	{axt_variant}::
822	A
823	{axl_motif}
824	representing a value that can take one of several distinct forms, where each form may have different structure.
825	Unlike
826	{axt_enumeration}
827	which constrains to predefined strings,
828	{axt_variant}
829	allows structurally different alternatives.
830	{axl_voicing_s}
831	define the specific forms and discrimination logic for their context.
832	
833	== Axial Record Terms
834	
835	{axr_record_s}
836	and
837	{axr_member_s}
838	provide format-agnostic patterns for structured data.
839	Format-specific variants (JSON, protobuf, XML, etc.) capture serialization-specific semantics.
840	{axr_member_s}
841	inherit format context from their parent
842	{axr_record}.
843	
844	[[axr_record]]
845	{axr_record}::
846	A
847	{axl_motif}
848	representing a named collection of typed
849	{axr_member_s}.
850	Abstract pattern that format-specific variants voice with serialization semantics.
851	Use format-specific variants (
852	{axr_record_json},
853	{axr_record_protobuf},
854	etc.) when format is known.
855	
856	[[axr_record_json]]
857	{axr_record_json}::
858	A
859	{axl_motif}
860	representing a JSON object structure.
861	{axr_member_s}
862	are unordered, string-keyed properties.
863	Requires
864	{axd_nullable}
865	specification since JSON has explicit null.
866	
867	[[axr_record_protobuf]]
868	{axr_record_protobuf}::
869	A
870	{axl_motif}
871	representing a protobuf message.
872	{axr_member_s}
873	require
874	{axd_field_number}
875	and
876	{axd_wire_type}
877	dimensions.
878	Supports schema evolution through field numbering.
879	
880	[[axr_record_xml]]
881	{axr_record_xml}::
882	A
883	{axl_motif}
884	representing an XML element structure.
885	{axr_member_s}
886	require
887	{axd_xml_form}
888	to distinguish attributes from child elements.
889	Order may be significant depending on schema.
890	
891	[[axr_record_yaml]]
892	{axr_record_yaml}::
893	A
894	{axl_motif}
895	representing a YAML mapping.
896	Superset of JSON with anchors, aliases, and multi-document support.
897	{axr_member_s}
898	follow JSON-like semantics with extended syntax.
899	
900	[[axr_record_binary]]
901	{axr_record_binary}::
902	A
903	{axl_motif}
904	representing a binary/memory structure.
905	{axr_member_s}
906	require
907	{axd_endianness}
908	for multi-byte fields.
909	Fixed layout with explicit size and alignment.
910	
911	[[axr_member]]
912	{axr_member}::
913	A
914	{axl_motif}
915	representing a typed element within an
916	{axr_record}.
917	Inherits serialization format from parent
918	{axr_record}.
919	Default 1-arity: reads one
920	{mcm_sprue}
921	from following text — the serialized property name (e.g., the JSON key).
922	The
923	{mcm_sprue}
924	is the exact wire-level name; the
925	{mcm_quoin}
926	is the conceptual name.
927	Additional type motifs on the annotation line (e.g.,
928	{axt_map},
929	{axt_string})
930	contribute their own arity to the total read sequence:
931	{axr_member}
932	sprue first, then type motif lookaheads.
933	Qualified by
934	{axd_dimension_s}
935	in
936	{axl_annotation}:
937	`//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axt_string`
938	+
939	Under
940	{axr_record_json},
941	exactly one of
942	{axd_property}
943	or
944	{axd_positional}
945	must qualify the
946	{axr_member}.
947	{axd_property}
948	preserves the 1-arity sprue contract.
949	{axd_positional}
950	suppresses it (0-arity for sprue) since the member's value provides the parent map's key directly and no separate sprue exists in the wire form.
951	
952	== Axial JSON Terms
953	
954	[[axj_structure]]
955	{axj_structure}::
956	A
957	{axl_motif}
958	representing named schema definition for JSON objects.
959	Defines complete object shape with field specifications.
960	
961	[[axj_field]]
962	{axj_field}::
963	A
964	{axl_motif}
965	representing named, typed element within a
966	{axj_structure}.
967	Combines tag identifier with type constraint.
968	
969	[[axj_field_tag]]
970	{axj_field_tag}::
971	A
972	{axl_motif}
973	representing the key identifier for a
974	{axj_field}.
975	Follows naming conventions of target specification.
976	
977	[[axj_field_type]]
978	{axj_field_type}::
979	A
980	{axl_motif}
981	representing the value constraint for a
982	{axj_field}.
983	Determines parsing and validation behavior.
984	
985	[[axj_array_element]]
986	{axj_array_element}::
987	A
988	{axl_motif}
989	representing type constraint for members of an
990	{axt_array}
991	field.
992	Applies uniformly to all array elements.

1117	[[axa_argument_list]]
1118	{axa_argument_list}::
1119	A
1120	{axl_motif}
1121	representing a structured section documenting an operation's arguments.
1122	{axl_voices}
1123	{axhopt_typed_parameter}
1124	/{axhopa_arg_parameter}
1125	in CLI context.
1126	Within an
1127	{axa_argument_list},
1128	{axd_dimension}
1129	annotations may precede bullet items to qualify argument usage without requiring anchors.
1130	Each item references a globally-defined argument term.

1133	Example section header definition:
1134	+
1135	[source,asciidoc]
1136	----

1149	//axd_required
1150	* {jjda_silks}

1163	+
1164	* The long form (e.g., `--file`)
1165	* The short form, or its absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")

1167	Example:
1168	+
1169	[source,asciidoc]
1170	----
1171	[[jjda_file]]
1172	//axl_voices axa_cli_option
1173	{jjda_file}::
1174	Path to the Gallops JSON file.

1181	{axa_exit_uniform}::
1182	A
1183	{axl_motif}
1184	representing an Exit Status section that follows standard Unix conventions.
1185	{axl_voices}
1186	{axhoc_completion}
1187	in CLI context.
1188	Exit 0 indicates success; any non-zero exit indicates error.
1189	{axl_voicing_s}
1190	may use terse notation since the pattern is conventional.
1191	The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
1192	Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
1193	+
1194	Example section header definition:
1195	+
1196	[source,asciidoc]
1197	----
1198	[[jjds_exit_uniform]]
1199	//axl_voices axa_exit_uniform
1200	{jjds_exit_uniform}::
1201	Section header for operations with standard exit semantics.
1202	----
1203	+
1204	Example usage:
1205	+
1206	[source,asciidoc]
1207	----
1208	{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
1209	----
1210	
1211	[[axa_exit_enumerated]]
1212	{axa_exit_enumerated}::
1213	A
1214	{axl_motif}
1215	representing an Exit Status section with distinct exit codes carrying specific meanings.
1216	{axl_voices}
1217	{axhoc_completion}
1218	in CLI context.
1219	{axl_voicing_s}
1220	require the definition text to list each exit code and its meaning.
1221	The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
1222	Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
1223	+
1224	Example section header definition:
1225	+
1226	[source,asciidoc]
1227	----
1228	[[jjds_exit_enumerated]]
1229	//axl_voices axa_exit_enumerated
1230	{jjds_exit_enumerated}::
1231	Section header for operations with distinct exit codes.
1232	----
1233	+
1234	Example usage:
1235	+
1236	[source,asciidoc]
1237	----
1238	{jjds_exit_enumerated}
1239	
1240	* 0 — found and valid
1241	* 1 — not found
1242	* 2 — found but malformed
1243	----

1433	== Axial Dimension Terms
1434	
1435	{axd_dimension_s}
1436	are modifiers that appear in
1437	{axl_annotation_s}
1438	to specify characteristics of members and arguments.
1439	Unlike primary
1440	{axl_motif_s},
1441	{axd_dimension_s}
1442	do not stand alone but qualify how a term voices its primary motif.
1443	Applicability of
1444	{axd_dimension_s}
1445	depends on serialization format context.
1446	
1447	[[axd_dimension]]
1448	{axd_dimension}::
1449	A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
1450	{axl_annotation}.
1451	{axd_dimension_s}
1452	appear after the primary
1453	{axl_motif}
1454	in annotation format: `//axl_voices primary dimension ...`
1455	
1456	=== Value Dimensions
1457	
1458	These
1459	{axd_dimension_s}
1460	describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
1461	They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).
1462	
1463	[[axd_required]]
1464	{axd_required}::
1465	Member or argument must be present.
1466	Absence triggers validation error or
1467	{axc_fatal}.
1468	
1469	[[axd_optional]]
1470	{axd_optional}::
1471	Member or argument may be omitted.
1472	Absence is valid and handled gracefully.
1473	
1474	[[axd_nullable]]
1475	{axd_nullable}::
1476	Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.
1477	Required for JSON where null is an explicit value type.
1478	Some formats (protobuf) conflate nullable with optional.
1479	
1480	[[axd_none]]
1481	{axd_none}::
1482	Section or field is explicitly declared as having no content.
1483	Distinguishes intentional absence from omission.
1484	Use in section annotations to mark compliance with required sections that have no applicable content.
1485	
1486	[[axd_defaulted]]
1487	{axd_defaulted}::
1488	Has fallback value when absent.
1489	Voicing should specify the default value in prose.
1490	
1491	[[axd_repeated]]
1492	{axd_repeated}::
1493	May have multiple values (array, list, repeated field).
1494	Absence typically means empty collection, not error.
1495	Applies to usage sites (arguments, members) where multiple occurrences are permitted.
1496	
1497	[[axd_list]]
1498	{axd_list}::
1499	Single value containing delimited multiple values.
1500	Distinct from
1501	{axd_repeated}
1502	which indicates multiple separate occurrences.
1503	Applies to type voicings where the type intrinsically represents a delimited collection.
1504	Voicing should specify the delimiter in prose (space-delimited, comma-delimited, etc.).
1505	
1506	=== Format-Specific Dimensions
1507	
1508	These
1509	{axd_dimension_s}
1510	apply only to specific serialization formats.
1511	Using them in forbidden contexts is a type-scheme error.
1512	
1513	[[axd_field_number]]
1514	{axd_field_number}::
1515	Numeric field identifier for protobuf encoding.
1516	Required for protobuf format.
1517	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
1518	
1519	[[axd_wire_type]]
1520	{axd_wire_type}::
1521	Protobuf encoding type (varint, fixed32, fixed64, length-delimited).
1522	Required for protobuf format.
1523	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
1524	
1525	[[axd_endianness]]
1526	{axd_endianness}::
1527	Byte order for binary encoding (big-endian, little-endian).
1528	Required for binary format.
1529	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, protobuf.
1530	
1531	[[axd_xml_form]]
1532	{axd_xml_form}::
1533	Whether XML member is attribute or child element.
1534	Required for XML format.
1535	Forbidden for JSON, YAML, protobuf, binary.
1536	
1537	[[axd_property]]
1538	{axd_property}::
1539	JSON
1540	{axr_member}
1541	encoded as a named property within its containing
1542	{axr_record_json}.
1543	The wire-level key is a fixed sprue declared at the definition site;
1544	the 1-arity sprue lookahead applies and is fulfilled by a backtick-delimited
1545	{mcm_sprue}
1546	in the following text.
1547	Required for JSON
1548	{axr_member_s}
1549	whose value sits at a named slot.
1550	Forbidden for protobuf, XML, YAML, binary.
1551	
1552	[[axd_positional]]
1553	{axd_positional}::
1554	JSON
1555	{axr_member}
1556	encoded by structural position rather than by a named property.
1557	The member's value provides the parent map's key directly;
1558	no separate sprue exists in the wire form.
1559	Suppresses the 1-arity sprue lookahead — under
1560	{axd_positional},
1561	{axr_member}
1562	becomes 0-arity for sprue.
1563	Required for JSON
1564	{axr_member_s}
1565	whose value is the parent map's key (e.g., a record's identity field encoded as the map entry's key).
1566	Forbidden for protobuf, XML, YAML, binary.
1567	
1568	Exactly one of
1569	{axd_property}
1570	or
1571	{axd_positional}
1572	must be present on every
1573	{axr_member}
1574	under an
1575	{axr_record_json}
1576	carrier whose value participates in the JSON wire form.
1577	Members that are computed, ephemeral, or stored outside the JSON wire are not
1578	{axr_member_s}
1579	of the
1580	{axr_record_json}
1581	and should not carry the
1582	{axr_member}
1583	voicing in the first place.
1584	
1585	[[axd_string]]
1586	{axd_string}::
1587	{axt_enum_value}
1588	encoded as a literal string token in the wire form.
1589	Carries a 1-arity sprue lookahead, fulfilled by a backtick-delimited
1590	{mcm_sprue}
1591	in the following text — the literal wire-level value as it appears in the carrier.
1592	The
1593	{mcm_sprue}
1594	is the exact wire-name; the
1595	{mcm_quoin}
1596	is the conceptual enum-value identity.
1597	Carrier-agnostic: applies wherever an
1598	{axt_enumeration}
1599	serializes its values as string tokens (JSON strings, bash regime values, YAML scalars, env-var values).
1600	Forbidden when the wire form is non-string (numeric, bitmask, object-tagged, positional, untagged variant) — those encodings will mint companion dimensions.
1601	
1602	=== Lifecycle Dimensions
1603	
1604	These
1605	{axd_dimension_s}
1606	qualify
1607	{axo_procedure}
1608	voicings to specify execution lifecycle.
1609	Exactly one lifecycle dimension is required for any
1610	{axo_procedure}
1611	voicing.
1612	Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.
1613	
1614	[[axd_transient]]
1615	{axd_transient}::
1616	{axo_procedure}
1617	executes and completes.
1618	The default lifecycle for most procedures.
1619	{axhoc_completion}
1620	documents exit status or return value.
1621	
1622	[[axd_longrunning]]
1623	{axd_longrunning}::
1624	{axo_procedure}
1625	runs continuously until explicitly stopped.
1626	{axhoc_completion}
1627	documents startup success criteria, not termination.
1628	Requires
1629	{axhos_step} (startup)
1630	and
1631	{axhos_step} (shutdown)
1632	sections.
1633	May include
1634	{axhos_step} (health, signals, recovery)
1635	sections.
1636	
1637	[[axd_periodic]]
1638	{axd_periodic}::
1639	{axo_procedure}
1640	executes on a schedule.
1641	{axhoc_completion}
1642	applies per-run.
1643	Requires
1644	{axhos_step} (schedule)
1645	section.
1646	May include
1647	{axhos_step} (idempotency, overlap)
1648	sections.
1649	
1650	=== Regime Dimensions
1651	
1652	These
1653	{axd_dimension_s}
1654	qualify regime structure annotations.
1655	
1656	[[axd_conditional]]
1657	{axd_conditional}::
1658	Group activation depends on one or more gate conditions.
1659	A group annotated with
1660	{axd_conditional}
1661	requires corresponding hierarchy markers in the subdocument to include at least one
1662	{axhrgc_gate}
1663	specifying the activation criteria.
1664	
1665	[[axrd_singleton]]
1666	{axrd_singleton}::
1667	Regime has exactly one assignment source.
1668	No enumeration or cross-instance operations apply.
1669	
1670	[[axrd_manifold]]
1671	{axrd_manifold}::
1672	Regime has multiple assignment sources (one per instance).
1673	Enumeration and cross-instance operations are meaningful.
1674	
1675	=== Protocol Dimensions
1676	
1677	These
1678	{axd_dimension_s}
1679	qualify
1680	{axi_protocol}
1681	voicings to specify transport, lifecycle, and concurrency presumptions of the bilateral discipline.
1682	
1683	[[axd_file_bus]]
1684	{axd_file_bus}::
1685	Transport dimension on an
1686	{axi_protocol}:
1687	participants communicate via a shared filesystem directory acting as the wire.
1688	The caller writes call envelopes as files; the callee observes atomic file appearance via rename(2) (write-temp-then-rename) and writes return envelopes the same way.
1689	No sockets, no in-process shared memory — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire.
1690	+
1691	*Index discipline:* participants coordinate transaction ordering via monotonic indices assigned by the caller and embedded in filenames or envelope bodies.
1692	Indices are session-local and reset on callee restart; the caller detects restart via index-collision or an explicit readiness exchange.
1693	Filename conventions for call and return envelopes are declared in the voicing spec, not in this motif.
1694	+
1695	*Atomicity:* every envelope write uses the rename(2) idiom so receivers never observe partial content.
1696	+
1697	*Wire-format neutrality:*
1698	{axd_file_bus}
1699	is transport-only; envelope contents are described by separate
1700	{axr_record_s}
1701	voicings (e.g.,
1702	{axr_record_json}).
1703	
1704	[[axd_disjoint_lifecycle]]
1705	{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}::
1706	Lifecycle dimension on an
1707	{axi_protocol}:
1708	the caller and callee have independent start and stop lifecycles.
1709	Neither actor can assume the other exists when it itself begins;
1710	each call must handle the possibility that the peer is absent, starting, or no longer the same instance that handled the previous call.
1711	+
1712	Implies the caller must observe callee presence before committing to a transaction, and must gracefully degrade or retry when the callee is unavailable.
1713	The specific observation mechanism (readiness probe, file-presence check, timeout) is declared in the protocol's detail site, not in this motif.
1714	+
1715	Contrasts with shared-session protocols where caller and callee share a bounded lifetime (e.g., an HTTP request-response pair scoped to a single process).
1716	
1717	=== Dimension Applicability
1718	
1719	Per-format guidance for
1720	{axd_dimension}
1721	usage:
1722	
1723	[cols="<,^,^,^,^,^",options="header"]
1724	|===
1725	|Dimension |JSON |Protobuf |XML |Binary |YAML
1726	
1727	|{axd_required}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
1728	|{axd_optional}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
1729	|{axd_nullable}     |required  |implicit  |recommend |context   |recommend
1730	|{axd_defaulted}    |optional  |required  |optional  |optional  |optional
1731	|{axd_repeated}     |recommend |required  |recommend |recommend |recommend
1732	|{axd_list}         |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional
1733	|{axd_field_number} |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
1734	|{axd_wire_type}    |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
1735	|{axd_endianness}   |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden
1736	|{axd_xml_form}     |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden
1737	|===
1738	
1739	Legend: required = must specify; recommend = should specify; optional = may specify; implicit = format handles; context = depends on usage; forbidden = not applicable.
1740

== Core Principles

The Axial Lexicon provides reusable, atomic terminology for technical specifications.
Each term maintains precise semantics while adapting to implementation context.
Specifications selectively import only needed axial terms, avoiding vocabulary bloat.

== Motif Vocabulary

AXL terms are
{axl_motif_s}
- abstract patterns that concrete specifications voice in their local context.

[[axl_motif]]
{axl_motif}::
An abstract pattern defined in the Axial Lexicon that can be voiced by concrete terms in specifications.
A
{axl_motif}
captures the essential shape of a concept while remaining independent of implementation context.
Specifications voice
{axl_motif_s}
through local terms that bind the abstract pattern to specific environments.

[[axl_voicing]]
{axl_voicing}::
A concrete term in a specification that gives local expression to A
{axl_motif}.
Each
{axl_voicing}
adapts the abstract pattern to a specific context while preserving the
{axl_motif_s}
essential character.

[[axl_voices]]
{axl_voices}::
The relationship where a concrete term expresses a
{axl_motif}
in a specific context.
When a term
{axl_voices}
a
{axl_motif},
it gives the abstract pattern local form and implementation-specific behavior.

[[axl_evokes]]
{axl_evokes}::
The relationship where A
{axl_motif}
calls forth its
{axl_voicing_s}
in the reader's understanding.
When encountering A
{axl_motif},
it
{axl_evokes}
the concrete realizations known from various specifications.

[[axl_annotation]]
{axl_annotation}::
A
{xref_MCM}
comment line that declares the
{axl_motif_s}
a term
{axl_voices}.
The
{axl_annotation}
appears between anchor and definition as a space-separated list of prefix terms on a comment line with no space after `//`.
+
Format: `//relationship primary [dimension ...]`
+
* First term: the relationship (typically `axl_voices`)
* Second term: the primary
{axl_motif}
being voiced
* Remaining terms: dimension terms (prefixed `axd_`) specifying characteristics
+
Examples:
+
* `//axl_voices axc_call` - simple voicing
* `//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_nullable` - voicing with dimensions
+
Category prefixes carry semantic meaning, eliminating need for punctuation.
Position determines role: relationship first, primary second, dimensions following.
This design prioritizes LLM interpretability over human visual parsing.
When ambiguity or friction arises during LLM processing, the format should evolve to resolve it.

== Motif Implications

{axl_motif_s}
carry implied characteristics beyond their definitions.
These implications guide how
{axl_voicing_s}
should behave:

* Anchor placement expectations per
{xref_MCM}
form patterns
* Type/instance level - where the
{axl_motif}
sits in abstraction hierarchy
* Contextual requirements - parent structures or operations the
{axl_motif}
exists within
* Voicing expectation - whether the
{axl_motif}
typically requires local voicing

== Form Expectations

Each AXL category implies an expected
{xref_MCM}
definition form pattern for documents using that category.
During type-scheme linting, these expectations guide validation of definition structure.

* {axc_*} control terms expect
{xref_MCM}
`mcm_form_deflist`
* {axj_structure} definitions expect
{xref_MCM}
`mcm_form_section`
* {axj_field} definitions expect
{xref_MCM}
`mcm_form_bullet` under parent structure
* {axk_premise} definitions expect
{xref_MCM}
`mcm_form_deflist`
* Most other categories expect
{xref_MCM}
`mcm_form_deflist`

These expectations support the
{axl_annotation}
pattern where documents include `//axl_voices motif ...` markers that are validated when AXL is in context.

== Axial Control Terms

[[axc_variable]]
{axc_variable}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a temporary value holder denoted by distinctive delimiters that exists for the duration of an operational sequence.
Implementation varies by context: shell variables in bash, dictionary entries in Python, sessionStorage in browser JavaScript.

[[axc_fatal]]
{axc_fatal}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing immediate termination of the operational sequence with error status, preventing all subsequent steps from executing.
Requires operator intervention or error recovery before retry.

[[axc_warn]]
{axc_warn}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing display of a non-blocking warning message while allowing the operational sequence to continue execution.
Alerts operators to concerning conditions without halting progress.

[[axc_store]]
{axc_store}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing capture of a value from an operation result or computation into a
{axc_variable}
for later reference.
Context determines storage mechanism while preserving semantic intent.

[[axc_use]]
{axc_use}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing reference to a previously stored
{axc_variable}
value in the current operation.
Retrieves stored values from context-appropriate storage location.

[[axc_require]]
{axc_require}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing validation check that confirms an expected condition, triggering
{axc_fatal}
or alternate execution path if not met.
Enforces preconditions and invariants throughout execution.

[[axc_poll]]
{axc_poll}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing repeated execution of a status check at defined intervals until completion condition or timeout.
Implements retry logic with configurable intervals and limits.

[[axc_call]]
{axc_call}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing direct invocation of an external service, API endpoint, or command.
Captures both success and error responses for processing.

[[axc_submit]]
{axc_submit}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing initiation of an asynchronous operation that returns immediately with a handle for later completion tracking.
Distinct from
{axc_call}
which expects synchronous response.

[[axc_await]]
{axc_await}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing blocking wait on a submitted asynchronous operation until completion.
Polls operation status until done, then returns final result or error.

[[axc_show]]
{axc_show}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing presentation of operational facts or values to the operator.
Implementation varies from console output to UI display based on context.

[[axc_step]]
{axc_step}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a normal progress message indicating successful completion of a processing stage.
Provides operational visibility without implying warnings or errors.

== Axial Environment Terms

{axe_environment_s}
specify execution contexts for control operations.
{axl_voicing_s}
of
{axc_call},
{axc_warn},
{axc_fatal}
and similar control motifs describe step-level behavior within operations.

[[axe_rest_api]]
{axe_rest_api}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing invocation of an HTTP REST API endpoint.
Request/response via JSON or other structured format.
Authentication via bearer tokens, API keys, or similar.
Calls return HTTP status codes and response bodies.

[[axe_console]]
{axe_console}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing direct terminal invocation.
Standard output and standard error are visible to the user.
Output can be routed directly to stdout/stderr.

[[axe_mcp_transport]]
{axe_mcp_transport}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing invocation through the MCP (Model Context Protocol) transport layer.
Standard error is swallowed by the transport — all output must be captured in a return buffer to reach the caller.

[[axe_daemon_runtime]]
{axe_daemon_runtime}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a long-running system process.
Errors are logged, not displayed.
Fatal means graceful shutdown with cleanup.
No human in immediate feedback loop.

== Axial Operation Terms

[[axo_procedure]]
{axo_procedure}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an executable operation with defined inputs, behavior, outputs, and completion semantics.
Standalone operations not affiliated with an entity.
Voiced at the
{axl_definition_site}
via
{axvo_procedure}.

[[axo_role]]
{axo_role}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an operational identity pattern that defines capabilities and permissions.
Determines available operations and access boundaries.

[[axo_identity]]
{axo_identity}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the abstract concept of an operational identity.
An entity capable of authentication and holding authorities within a system.
{axo_identity}
is the pattern;
{axo_actor_s}
are named instantiations with specific bindings.

[[axo_actor]]
{axo_actor}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a specific named identity with defined responsibilities and exclusive authority over an operational domain.
{axo_actor_s}
instantiate
{axo_identity}
with concrete bindings (human via OAuth, service via service account) and permissions.

[[axo_entity]]
{axo_entity}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an object with both structure and behavior.
Unlike
{axr_record}
which captures pure data,
{axo_entity}
includes methods, builder patterns, or lifecycle operations.
{axl_voicing_s}
describe the entity's data members and its behavioral interface (construction, mutation, registration).
Typical manifestations include Rust structs with `impl` blocks, builder-pattern APIs, or objects that participate in registration protocols.

== Axial Type Terms

[[axt_type]]
{axt_type}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a data format constraint that defines valid values and structures.
Enforces data validation and enables type-safe operations.

[[axt_string]]
{axt_string}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing text data type with optional pattern validation.
{axl_voicing_s}
may address context-specific concerns including:
+
* Encoding (ASCII, UTF-8, locale-dependent character sets)
* Termination semantics (null-terminated, length-prefixed, delimiter-bounded)
* Mutability (immutable values, mutable buffers)
* Memory management (owned, borrowed, reference-counted)
* Safety constraints (shell-safe, URL-safe, JSON-safe, SQL-safe)
* Format patterns (timestamps, identifiers, hashes, paths)
* Size and bounds (fixed-length, maximum length, unbounded)

[[axt_number]]
{axt_number}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing numeric value type supporting integers and floating point.
May specify range constraints or precision requirements.

[[axt_boolean]]
{axt_boolean}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing binary true/false value type.
Maps to language-specific boolean implementations.

[[axt_array]]
{axt_array}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing ordered collection of typed elements.
Specifies element type and optional size constraints.

[[axt_object]]
{axt_object}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing named field collection forming a composite type.
Contains typed fields with defined cardinality.

[[axt_map]]
{axt_map}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a dictionary structure with typed keys and typed values.
2-arity: reads two
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
from following text — the first identifies the key type, the second identifies the value type.
Keys are unique within the map; values are accessed by key lookup.
Serialization format determines key ordering guarantees and key type constraints (e.g., JSON restricts keys to strings).

[[axt_enumeration]]
{axt_enumeration}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing constrained set of valid string values.
Restricts values to predefined options.

[[axt_enum_value]]
{axt_enum_value}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing single allowed option within an
{axt_enumeration}.
Must be explicitly listed in enumeration definition.
+
Encoding is carried by an
{axd_dimension}
on the voicing line — e.g.,
{axd_string}
for string-token-encoded values.
The encoding
{axd_dimension}
specifies the
{mcm_sprue}
arity for the wire-level value.
Exactly one encoding dimension must qualify every
{axt_enum_value}
voicing whose
{axt_enumeration}
participates in a wire form.

[[axt_insignia]]
{axt_insignia}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a glyph-prefixed typed identity string with seed-based allocation.
Extends
{axt_string}
with structural properties: a unicode verification glyph (decorative in input, mandatory in output), a fixed encoding format, a monotonic seed allocation mechanism, and optional hierarchical embedding (parent identity encoded as a prefix substring).
+
Every
{axt_insignia}
declares an encoding nature via
{axd_dimension_s}:
{axd_algebraic}
or
{axd_temporal}.

[[axt_variant]]
{axt_variant}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a value that can take one of several distinct forms, where each form may have different structure.
Unlike
{axt_enumeration}
which constrains to predefined strings,
{axt_variant}
allows structurally different alternatives.
{axl_voicing_s}
define the specific forms and discrimination logic for their context.

== Axial Record Terms

{axr_record_s}
and
{axr_member_s}
provide format-agnostic patterns for structured data.
Format-specific variants (JSON, protobuf, XML, etc.) capture serialization-specific semantics.
{axr_member_s}
inherit format context from their parent
{axr_record}.

[[axr_record]]
{axr_record}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a named collection of typed
{axr_member_s}.
Abstract pattern that format-specific variants voice with serialization semantics.
Use format-specific variants (
{axr_record_json},
{axr_record_protobuf},
etc.) when format is known.

[[axr_record_json]]
{axr_record_json}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a JSON object structure.
{axr_member_s}
are unordered, string-keyed properties.
Requires
{axd_nullable}
specification since JSON has explicit null.

[[axr_record_protobuf]]
{axr_record_protobuf}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a protobuf message.
{axr_member_s}
require
{axd_field_number}
and
{axd_wire_type}
dimensions.
Supports schema evolution through field numbering.

[[axr_record_xml]]
{axr_record_xml}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an XML element structure.
{axr_member_s}
require
{axd_xml_form}
to distinguish attributes from child elements.
Order may be significant depending on schema.

[[axr_record_yaml]]
{axr_record_yaml}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a YAML mapping.
Superset of JSON with anchors, aliases, and multi-document support.
{axr_member_s}
follow JSON-like semantics with extended syntax.

[[axr_record_binary]]
{axr_record_binary}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a binary/memory structure.
{axr_member_s}
require
{axd_endianness}
for multi-byte fields.
Fixed layout with explicit size and alignment.

[[axr_member]]
{axr_member}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a typed element within an
{axr_record}.
Inherits serialization format from parent
{axr_record}.
Default 1-arity: reads one
{mcm_sprue}
from following text — the serialized property name (e.g., the JSON key).
The
{mcm_sprue}
is the exact wire-level name; the
{mcm_quoin}
is the conceptual name.
Additional type motifs on the annotation line (e.g.,
{axt_map},
{axt_string})
contribute their own arity to the total read sequence:
{axr_member}
sprue first, then type motif lookaheads.
Qualified by
{axd_dimension_s}
in
{axl_annotation}:
`//axl_voices axr_member axd_required axt_string`
+
Under
{axr_record_json},
exactly one of
{axd_property}
or
{axd_positional}
must qualify the
{axr_member}.
{axd_property}
preserves the 1-arity sprue contract.
{axd_positional}
suppresses it (0-arity for sprue) since the member's value provides the parent map's key directly and no separate sprue exists in the wire form.

== Axial JSON Terms

[[axj_structure]]
{axj_structure}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing named schema definition for JSON objects.
Defines complete object shape with field specifications.

[[axj_field]]
{axj_field}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing named, typed element within a
{axj_structure}.
Combines tag identifier with type constraint.

[[axj_field_tag]]
{axj_field_tag}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the key identifier for a
{axj_field}.
Follows naming conventions of target specification.

[[axj_field_type]]
{axj_field_type}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the value constraint for a
{axj_field}.
Determines parsing and validation behavior.

[[axj_array_element]]
{axj_array_element}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing type constraint for members of an
{axt_array}
field.
Applies uniformly to all array elements.

[[axj_nested_field]]
{axj_nested_field}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing fields within object-typed fields enabling hierarchy.
Maintains type safety through nested validation.

[[axj_root_structure]]
{axj_root_structure}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing top-level JSON object schema.
Entry point for JSON document validation.

[[axj_field_cardinality]]
{axj_field_cardinality}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing required, optional, or repeated nature of a field.
Maps to validation rules in implementation.

[[axj_type_reference]]
{axj_type_reference}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing pointer to another
{axj_structure}
for composition.
Maintains referential integrity across definitions.

== Axial Argument Terms

[[axa_argument]]
{axa_argument}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing command parameter accepting a value.
Foundation for CLI and function interfaces.

[[axa_positional]]
{axa_positional}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing order-determines-meaning parameter in command invocation.
Position in argument list defines semantic role.

[[axa_keyword]]
{axa_keyword}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing name-determines-meaning parameter with explicit identifier.
Order-independent with explicit naming.

[[axa_long_option]]
{axa_long_option}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing keyword argument with `--` prefix following GNU conventions.
Uses hyphenated multi-word names.

[[axa_short_option]]
{axa_short_option}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing keyword argument with single `-` prefix for brevity.
Often provides shorthand for
{axa_long_option}.

[[axa_option_name]]
{axa_option_name}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the identifier portion of a keyword argument.
Defines the semantic meaning of the option.

[[axa_option_value]]
{axa_option_value}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the data portion following an option identifier.
Subject to
{axa_argument_type}
constraints.

[[axa_argument_type]]
{axa_argument_type}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing expected data format for an
{axa_argument}.
Enforces type safety at interface boundary.

[[axa_required]]
{axa_required}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing attribute indicating an
{axa_argument}
must be provided.
Absence triggers
{axc_fatal}
or error response.

[[axa_optional]]
{axa_optional}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing attribute indicating an
{axa_argument}
may be omitted.
Provides flexibility in interface usage.

[[axa_defaulted]]
{axa_defaulted}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing attribute indicating an
{axa_argument}
has a fallback value.
Specifies behavior when
{axa_optional}
argument omitted.

[[axa_argument_list]]
{axa_argument_list}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a structured section documenting an operation's arguments.
{axl_voices}
{axhopt_typed_parameter}
/{axhopa_arg_parameter}
in CLI context.
Within an
{axa_argument_list},
{axd_dimension}
annotations may precede bullet items to qualify argument usage without requiring anchors.
Each item references a globally-defined argument term.
The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
+
Example section header definition:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
[[jjds_arguments]]
//axl_voices axa_argument_list
{jjds_arguments}::
Section header introducing operation arguments.
----
+
Example usage in an operation:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
{jjds_arguments}

//axd_required
* {jjda_silks}

//axd_optional axd_defaulted
* {jjda_file}
----

[[axa_cli_option]]
{axa_cli_option}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a command-line option definition.
{axl_voicing_s}
require the definition text to specify:
+
* The long form (e.g., `--file`)
* The short form, or its absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")
+
Example:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
[[jjda_file]]
//axl_voices axa_cli_option
{jjda_file}::
Path to the Gallops JSON file.
Long: `--file`.
Short: none.
Default: `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
----

[[axa_exit_uniform]]
{axa_exit_uniform}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an Exit Status section that follows standard Unix conventions.
{axl_voices}
{axhoc_completion}
in CLI context.
Exit 0 indicates success; any non-zero exit indicates error.
{axl_voicing_s}
may use terse notation since the pattern is conventional.
The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
+
Example section header definition:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
[[jjds_exit_uniform]]
//axl_voices axa_exit_uniform
{jjds_exit_uniform}::
Section header for operations with standard exit semantics.
----
+
Example usage:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
----

[[axa_exit_enumerated]]
{axa_exit_enumerated}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an Exit Status section with distinct exit codes carrying specific meanings.
{axl_voices}
{axhoc_completion}
in CLI context.
{axl_voicing_s}
require the definition text to list each exit code and its meaning.
The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
+
Example section header definition:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
[[jjds_exit_enumerated]]
//axl_voices axa_exit_enumerated
{jjds_exit_enumerated}::
Section header for operations with distinct exit codes.
----
+
Example usage:
+
[source,asciidoc]
----
{jjds_exit_enumerated}

* 0 — found and valid
* 1 — not found
* 2 — found but malformed
----

== Axial Interface Terms

[[axi_command_interface]]
{axi_command_interface}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing complete specification of a command's invocation pattern.
Defines syntax and semantics for command usage.

[[axi_subcommand]]
{axi_subcommand}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing nested command within parent command structure.
Inherits context from parent command.

[[axi_command_signature]]
{axi_command_signature}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing complete parameter specification for a command.
Serves as contract for command invocation.

[[axi_endpoint]]
{axi_endpoint}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing URL path accepting HTTP requests.
Defines resource location and accepted operations.

[[axi_api_method]]
{axi_api_method}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing HTTP verb combined with
{axi_endpoint}
specification.
Includes request and response specifications.

[[axi_url_parameter]]
{axi_url_parameter}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing query string key-value pair in URL.
Appears after `?` in URL structure.

[[axi_hash_parameter]]
{axi_hash_parameter}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing fragment identifier for client-side state.
Persists state without server round-trips.

[[axi_request_body]]
{axi_request_body}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing structured input payload for HTTP requests.
Validated against
{axj_structure}
definitions.

[[axi_response_body]]
{axi_response_body}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing structured output payload from HTTP responses.
Conforms to documented
{axj_structure}.

[[axi_cli_interface]]
{axi_cli_interface}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing command-line invocation pattern specification.
Documents
{axa_argument_s}
and behavior.

[[axi_cli_program]]
{axi_cli_program}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Unix-style command-line program.
Invoked from a shell with arguments.
Writes results to stdout, errors to stderr.
Returns exit status: 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
Suitable for scripted, non-interactive use.

[[axi_cli_command_group]]
{axi_cli_command_group}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a namespace within a CLI that contains subcommands.
Does not perform operations itself; serves as organizational container.
May be nested within an
{axi_cli_program}
or another
{axi_cli_command_group}.
Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent.

[[axi_cli_subcommand]]
{axi_cli_subcommand}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a leaf operation within an
{axi_cli_program}
or
{axi_cli_command_group}.
Performs an actual operation; not a container for other commands.
Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent: arguments, stdout, stderr, exit status.

[[axi_rest_api]]
{axi_rest_api}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing resource-based web service interface.
Maps operations to HTTP methods and resources.

[[axi_rpc_interface]]
{axi_rpc_interface}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing remote procedure call pattern for service invocation.
Emphasizes operation semantics over HTTP methods.

[[axi_sse_endpoint]]
{axi_sse_endpoint}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing server-sent event stream for push notifications.
Enables real-time updates to connected clients.

[[axi_cc_slash_command]]
{axi_cc_slash_command}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Claude Code slash command skill artifact.
Implemented as a `.claude/commands/*.md` file containing orchestration logic that the LLM executes as a skill.
Slash commands coordinate multiple
{axi_cli_subcommand}
invocations, gather context, and apply workflow policies.

[[axi_cc_claudemd_verb]]
{axi_cc_claudemd_verb}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an action verb defined in a
{axi_cc_claudemd_section}
that maps user intent to tool-layer operations.
The verb exists as a concept in the LLM context — either mapping directly to a single
{axi_cli_subcommand}
(direct verb) or orchestrating multiple operations (multistep verb implemented by a
{axi_cc_slash_command}).

[[axi_cc_claudemd_section]]
{axi_cc_claudemd_section}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a managed section within a project CLAUDE.md file.
Provides persistent LLM context that bridges specification-layer concepts to runtime execution.
Typically contains command references, vocabulary mappings, and operational guidance.

[[axi_protocol]]
{axi_protocol}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the bilateral discipline binding two
{axo_actor_s}
— a caller and a callee — through a defined sequence of call and return envelopes.
Distinct from
{axi_rpc_interface}
which describes one side's view of a service;
{axi_protocol}
is the relationship itself, owning transport, lifecycle, and concurrency presumptions.
A protocol quoin carries transport via
{axd_dimension_s}
(e.g.,
{axd_file_bus}),
lifecycle presumptions (e.g.,
{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}),
and concurrency discipline as dimensions on the primary voicing.
Structural detail — the roster of transactions, call/return envelopes, and member types — lives at the
{axl_detail_site}
under
{axhpb_protocol}
and nested hierarchy markers.

== Axial Dimension Terms

{axd_dimension_s}
are modifiers that appear in
{axl_annotation_s}
to specify characteristics of members and arguments.
Unlike primary
{axl_motif_s},
{axd_dimension_s}
do not stand alone but qualify how a term voices its primary motif.
Applicability of
{axd_dimension_s}
depends on serialization format context.

[[axd_dimension]]
{axd_dimension}::
A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
{axl_annotation}.
{axd_dimension_s}
appear after the primary
{axl_motif}
in annotation format: `//axl_voices primary dimension ...`

=== Value Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).

[[axd_required]]
{axd_required}::
Member or argument must be present.
Absence triggers validation error or
{axc_fatal}.

[[axd_optional]]
{axd_optional}::
Member or argument may be omitted.
Absence is valid and handled gracefully.

[[axd_nullable]]
{axd_nullable}::
Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.
Required for JSON where null is an explicit value type.
Some formats (protobuf) conflate nullable with optional.

[[axd_none]]
{axd_none}::
Section or field is explicitly declared as having no content.
Distinguishes intentional absence from omission.
Use in section annotations to mark compliance with required sections that have no applicable content.

[[axd_defaulted]]
{axd_defaulted}::
Has fallback value when absent.
Voicing should specify the default value in prose.

[[axd_repeated]]
{axd_repeated}::
May have multiple values (array, list, repeated field).
Absence typically means empty collection, not error.
Applies to usage sites (arguments, members) where multiple occurrences are permitted.

[[axd_list]]
{axd_list}::
Single value containing delimited multiple values.
Distinct from
{axd_repeated}
which indicates multiple separate occurrences.
Applies to type voicings where the type intrinsically represents a delimited collection.
Voicing should specify the delimiter in prose (space-delimited, comma-delimited, etc.).

=== Format-Specific Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
apply only to specific serialization formats.
Using them in forbidden contexts is a type-scheme error.

[[axd_field_number]]
{axd_field_number}::
Numeric field identifier for protobuf encoding.
Required for protobuf format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.

[[axd_wire_type]]
{axd_wire_type}::
Protobuf encoding type (varint, fixed32, fixed64, length-delimited).
Required for protobuf format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.

[[axd_endianness]]
{axd_endianness}::
Byte order for binary encoding (big-endian, little-endian).
Required for binary format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, protobuf.

[[axd_xml_form]]
{axd_xml_form}::
Whether XML member is attribute or child element.
Required for XML format.
Forbidden for JSON, YAML, protobuf, binary.

[[axd_property]]
{axd_property}::
JSON
{axr_member}
encoded as a named property within its containing
{axr_record_json}.
The wire-level key is a fixed sprue declared at the definition site;
the 1-arity sprue lookahead applies and is fulfilled by a backtick-delimited
{mcm_sprue}
in the following text.
Required for JSON
{axr_member_s}
whose value sits at a named slot.
Forbidden for protobuf, XML, YAML, binary.

[[axd_positional]]
{axd_positional}::
JSON
{axr_member}
encoded by structural position rather than by a named property.
The member's value provides the parent map's key directly;
no separate sprue exists in the wire form.
Suppresses the 1-arity sprue lookahead — under
{axd_positional},
{axr_member}
becomes 0-arity for sprue.
Required for JSON
{axr_member_s}
whose value is the parent map's key (e.g., a record's identity field encoded as the map entry's key).
Forbidden for protobuf, XML, YAML, binary.

Exactly one of
{axd_property}
or
{axd_positional}
must be present on every
{axr_member}
under an
{axr_record_json}
carrier whose value participates in the JSON wire form.
Members that are computed, ephemeral, or stored outside the JSON wire are not
{axr_member_s}
of the
{axr_record_json}
and should not carry the
{axr_member}
voicing in the first place.

[[axd_string]]
{axd_string}::
{axt_enum_value}
encoded as a literal string token in the wire form.
Carries a 1-arity sprue lookahead, fulfilled by a backtick-delimited
{mcm_sprue}
in the following text — the literal wire-level value as it appears in the carrier.
The
{mcm_sprue}
is the exact wire-name; the
{mcm_quoin}
is the conceptual enum-value identity.
Carrier-agnostic: applies wherever an
{axt_enumeration}
serializes its values as string tokens (JSON strings, bash regime values, YAML scalars, env-var values).
Forbidden when the wire form is non-string (numeric, bitmask, object-tagged, positional, untagged variant) — those encodings will mint companion dimensions.

=== Lifecycle Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
qualify
{axo_procedure}
voicings to specify execution lifecycle.
Exactly one lifecycle dimension is required for any
{axo_procedure}
voicing.
Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.

[[axd_transient]]
{axd_transient}::
{axo_procedure}
executes and completes.
The default lifecycle for most procedures.
{axhoc_completion}
documents exit status or return value.

[[axd_longrunning]]
{axd_longrunning}::
{axo_procedure}
runs continuously until explicitly stopped.
{axhoc_completion}
documents startup success criteria, not termination.
Requires
{axhos_step} (startup)
and
{axhos_step} (shutdown)
sections.
May include
{axhos_step} (health, signals, recovery)
sections.

[[axd_periodic]]
{axd_periodic}::
{axo_procedure}
executes on a schedule.
{axhoc_completion}
applies per-run.
Requires
{axhos_step} (schedule)
section.
May include
{axhos_step} (idempotency, overlap)
sections.

=== Regime Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
qualify regime structure annotations.

[[axd_conditional]]
{axd_conditional}::
Group activation depends on one or more gate conditions.
A group annotated with
{axd_conditional}
requires corresponding hierarchy markers in the subdocument to include at least one
{axhrgc_gate}
specifying the activation criteria.

[[axrd_singleton]]
{axrd_singleton}::
Regime has exactly one assignment source.
No enumeration or cross-instance operations apply.

[[axrd_manifold]]
{axrd_manifold}::
Regime has multiple assignment sources (one per instance).
Enumeration and cross-instance operations are meaningful.

=== Protocol Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
qualify
{axi_protocol}
voicings to specify transport, lifecycle, and concurrency presumptions of the bilateral discipline.

[[axd_file_bus]]
{axd_file_bus}::
Transport dimension on an
{axi_protocol}:
participants communicate via a shared filesystem directory acting as the wire.
The caller writes call envelopes as files; the callee observes atomic file appearance via rename(2) (write-temp-then-rename) and writes return envelopes the same way.
No sockets, no in-process shared memory — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire.
+
*Index discipline:* participants coordinate transaction ordering via monotonic indices assigned by the caller and embedded in filenames or envelope bodies.
Indices are session-local and reset on callee restart; the caller detects restart via index-collision or an explicit readiness exchange.
Filename conventions for call and return envelopes are declared in the voicing spec, not in this motif.
+
*Atomicity:* every envelope write uses the rename(2) idiom so receivers never observe partial content.
+
*Wire-format neutrality:*
{axd_file_bus}
is transport-only; envelope contents are described by separate
{axr_record_s}
voicings (e.g.,
{axr_record_json}).

[[axd_disjoint_lifecycle]]
{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}::
Lifecycle dimension on an
{axi_protocol}:
the caller and callee have independent start and stop lifecycles.
Neither actor can assume the other exists when it itself begins;
each call must handle the possibility that the peer is absent, starting, or no longer the same instance that handled the previous call.
+
Implies the caller must observe callee presence before committing to a transaction, and must gracefully degrade or retry when the callee is unavailable.
The specific observation mechanism (readiness probe, file-presence check, timeout) is declared in the protocol's detail site, not in this motif.
+
Contrasts with shared-session protocols where caller and callee share a bounded lifetime (e.g., an HTTP request-response pair scoped to a single process).

=== Dimension Applicability

Per-format guidance for
{axd_dimension}
usage:

[cols="<,^,^,^,^,^",options="header"]
|===
|Dimension |JSON |Protobuf |XML |Binary |YAML

|{axd_required}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_optional}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_nullable}     |required  |implicit  |recommend |context   |recommend
|{axd_defaulted}    |optional  |required  |optional  |optional  |optional
|{axd_repeated}     |recommend |required  |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_list}         |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional
|{axd_field_number} |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
|{axd_wire_type}    |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
|{axd_endianness}   |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden
|{axd_xml_form}     |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden
|===

Legend: required = must specify; recommend = should specify; optional = may specify; implicit = format handles; context = depends on usage; forbidden = not applicable.
