1741	== Axial Format Terms
1742	
1743	{axf_format_s}
1744	specify how configuration data is serialized and carried.
1745	{axrg_assignment_s}
1746	use a particular
1747	{axf_format}
1748	to encode
1749	{axrg_variable}
1750	values in files that can be sourced, included, or parsed.
1751	
1752	[[axf_format]]
1753	{axf_format}::
1754	A
1755	{axl_motif}
1756	representing a serialization encoding for configuration data.
1757	{axf_format_s}
1758	determine how
1759	{axrg_assignment_s}
1760	are written to and read from files.
1761	
1762	[[axf_bash]]
1763	{axf_bash}::
1764	A
1765	{axl_motif}
1766	representing bash script format for configuration.
1767	Files are source-able via `. file` or `source file`.
1768	Variables declared as `VAR=value` without export, or `export VAR=value` for environment propagation.
1769	
1770	[[axf_makefile]]
1771	{axf_makefile}::
1772	A
1773	{axl_motif}
1774	representing makefile format for configuration.
1775	Files are includable via `include file`.
1776	Variables declared as `VAR := value` for immediate expansion or `VAR = value` for deferred expansion.
1777	
1778	[[axf_json]]
1779	{axf_json}::
1780	A
1781	{axl_motif}
1782	representing JSON format for configuration.
1783	Standard JSON object structure with string keys mapping to typed values.
1784	Parsed by standard JSON libraries.
1785	
1786	[[axf_yaml]]
1787	{axf_yaml}::
1788	A
1789	{axl_motif}
1790	representing YAML format for configuration.
1791	Superset of JSON with additional syntax for readability.
1792	Parsed by YAML libraries with attention to version-specific semantics.
1793	
1794	== Axial Regime Terms
1795	
1796	{axrg_regime_s}
1797	are structured configuration systems that coordinate specification, validation, and rendering of typed configuration data.
1798	
1799	[[axrg_regime]]
1800	{axrg_regime}::
1801	A
1802	{axl_motif}
1803	representing a structured configuration system.
1804	A
1805	{axrg_regime}
1806	binds related
1807	{axrg_variable_s}
1808	under a namespace prefix, with coordinated specification, validation, and rendering components.
1809	The specification defines what can be configured;
1810	{axrg_assignment_s}
1811	provide specific values.
1812	
1813	[[axrg_variable]]
1814	{axrg_variable}::
1815	A
1816	{axl_motif}
1817	representing a named configuration element at schema level.
1818	Each
1819	{axrg_variable}
1820	belongs to an
1821	{axrg_regime}
1822	and defines a name, type constraints, and relationships with other variables.
1823	{axrg_variable_s}
1824	are the "what can be configured" definitions;
1825	{axrg_assignment_s}
1826	bind values to them.
1827	
1828	[[axrg_assignment]]
1829	{axrg_assignment}::
1830	A
1831	{axl_motif}
1832	representing a specific value bound to an
1833	{axrg_variable}.
1834	{axrg_assignment_s}
1835	are carried in files of a particular
1836	{axf_format}
1837	(bash, makefile, JSON, etc.).
1838	Multiple assignment files may exist for the same
1839	{axrg_regime},
1840	each providing different configuration instances.
1841	
1842	[[axrg_prefix]]
1843	{axrg_prefix}::
1844	A
1845	{axl_motif}
1846	representing the stable character sequence that namespaces all
1847	{axrg_variable_s}
1848	within an
1849	{axrg_regime}.
1850	Typically composed of a project marker and regime marker (e.g., `RBRR_` = `RB` + `RR` + `_`).
1851	Once established, an
1852	{axrg_prefix}
1853	becomes deeply embedded across code, configuration files, and documentation.
1854	Changing an
1855	{axrg_prefix}
1856	requires coordinated refactoring across all consuming artifacts.
1857	
1858	== Axial Regime Provenance Dimensions
1859	
1860	Provenance dimensions qualify
1861	{axrg_assignment}
1862	to declare how values arrive at
1863	{axvr_kindle}
1864	time.
1865	These are valid dimensions on
1866	{axvr_regime}
1867	declaring which provenance(s) a regime supports.
1868	
1869	[[axrd_file_sourced]]
1870	{axrd_file_sourced}::
1871	Assignments loaded from a persistent file via source or equivalent mechanism.
1872	The file path is known at load time and the regime module is responsible for locating and sourcing it.
1873	
1874	[[axrd_env_inherited]]
1875	{axrd_env_inherited}::
1876	Assignments received as environment variables from a parent process.
1877	The regime module finds variables already in scope; no file sourcing occurs.
1878	
1879	[[axrd_constructed]]
1880	{axrd_constructed}::
1881	Assignments computed or set programmatically before kindle.
1882	Values are assembled by application logic rather than loaded from a static source.
1883	
1884	== Axial Regime Annotation Terms
1885	
1886	Regime annotations use the prefix-discriminated form (`//ax...` with no space after `//`) to provide structured metadata about regime definitions in AsciiDoc specifications.
1887	Two complementary annotation families enable regime specification across parent documents and their subdocuments.
1888	
1889	=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvr_*)
1890	
1891	{axvr_*} annotations appear in parent documents between anchor and definition, declaring the regime role of the term being defined.
1892	The surface syntax follows the prefix-discriminated pattern:
1893	
1894	[source,asciidoc]
1895	----
1896	[[rbrv_sigil]]
1897	//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
1898	{rbrv_sigil}::
1899	Vessel identifier for build configurations.
1900	----
1901	
1902	The annotation line starts with `//axvr_` (no space after `//`), followed by the specific voicing term, then space-separated dimension and type terms.
1903	The anchor immediately preceding the annotation must match the first
1904	{mcm_attribute_reference}
1905	in the definition text that follows.
1906	
1907	[[axvr_regime]]
1908	{axvr_regime}::
1909	Voices a regime definition.
1910	The annotated term defines a structured configuration system that binds related
1911	{axrg_variable_s}
1912	under a namespace prefix.
1913	+
1914	{axd_dimension_s}:
1915	{axf_format}
1916	(specifying how
1917	{axrg_assignment_s}
1918	are carried).
1919	
1920	[[axvr_variable]]
1921	{axvr_variable}::
1922	Voices a regime variable definition.
1923	The annotated term defines a named configuration element within a regime.
1924	The definition text must include, as its second
1925	{mcm_attribute_reference},
1926	the parent regime (an anchor that voices
1927	{axrg_regime}).
1928	This ties each variable unequivocally to its regime.
1929	+
1930	{axd_dimension_s}:
1931	{axd_required}
1932	or
1933	{axd_optional},
1934	type
1935	{axl_motif}
1936	(base type or type voicing).
1937	
1938	[[axvr_group]]
1939	{axvr_group}::
1940	Voices a regime group definition.
1941	The annotated term defines a named grouping of related
1942	{axrg_variable_s}
1943	within a regime.
1944	The definition text must include, as its second
1945	{mcm_attribute_reference},
1946	the parent regime (an anchor that voices
1947	{axrg_regime}).
1948	+
1949	{axd_dimension_s}:
1950	{axd_conditional}
1951	(group activation depends on gate conditions in subdocument).
1952	
1953	[[axvr_kindle]]
1954	{axvr_kindle}::
1955	Voices a regime kindle operation definition.
1956	The annotated term is a dedicated
1957	{mcm_quoin}
1958	naming the silent initialization phase for exactly one
1959	{axrg_regime}:
1960	setting defaults, detecting unexpected variables, and building derived structures.
1961	
1962	[[axvr_validate]]
1963	{axvr_validate}::
1964	Voices a regime validate operation definition.
1965	The annotated term is a dedicated
1966	{mcm_quoin}
1967	naming the strict verification phase for exactly one
1968	{axrg_regime}:
1969	checking all type and constraint requirements, failing on first violation.
1970	
1971	[[axvr_render]]
1972	{axvr_render}::
1973	Voices a regime render operation definition.
1974	The annotated term is a dedicated
1975	{mcm_quoin}
1976	naming the diagnostic display phase for exactly one
1977	{axrg_regime}:
1978	producing human-readable presentation of current state.
1979	Rendering occurs before validation so all fields are visible on failure.
1980	
1981	[[axvr_list]]
1982	{axvr_list}::
1983	Voices a regime list operation definition.
1984	The annotated term is a dedicated
1985	{mcm_quoin}
1986	naming the enumeration operation for exactly one
1987	{axrd_manifold}
1988	{axrg_regime}:
1989	listing available instances.
1990	
1991	[[axvr_survey]]
1992	{axvr_survey}::
1993	Voices a regime survey operation definition.
1994	The annotated term is a dedicated
1995	{mcm_quoin}
1996	naming the read-only cross-instance summary operation for exactly one
1997	{axrd_manifold}
1998	{axrg_regime}.
1999	
2000	[[axvr_audit]]
2001	{axvr_audit}::
2002	Voices a regime audit operation definition.
2003	The annotated term is a dedicated
2004	{mcm_quoin}
2005	naming the cross-instance validation plus external verification operation for exactly one
2006	{axrd_manifold}
2007	{axrg_regime}.
2008	
2009	=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhr*_)
2010	
2011	Hierarchy markers appear in regime subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
2012	Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
2013	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2014	from the following text to establish hierarchical context.
2015	
2016	The surface syntax:
2017	
2018	[source,asciidoc]
2019	----
2020	//axhrb_regime
2021	{rbrv_regime}
2022	
2023	//axhrgv_variable
2024	{rbrv_sigil}
2025	----
2026	
2027	The hierarchy marker line starts with `//axhr` (no space after `//`), followed by the sub-prefix identifying the marker type.
2028	Following text contains the
2029	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2030	that the marker reads ahead.
2031	
2032	==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
2033	
2034	[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
2035	|===
2036	|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
2037	
2038	|{axhrb_regime}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The regime this subdoc describes
2039	|{axhrv_variable}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An ungrouped regime variable
2040	|{axhrgb_group}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A group (must match {axvr_group} in parent)
2041	|{axhrgc_gate}      |2 {mcm_attribute_reference_s}|Enumerated variable + enum value
2042	|{axhrgv_variable}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A variable within current group
2043	|{axhro_kindle}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level kindle operation for this regime
2044	|{axhro_validate}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level validate operation for this regime
2045	|{axhro_render}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level render operation for this regime
2046	|{axhro_list}       |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level list operation for this regime
2047	|{axhro_survey}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level survey operation for this regime
2048	|{axhro_audit}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level audit operation for this regime
2049	|===
2050	
2051	[[axhrb_regime]]
2052	{axhrb_regime}::
2053	Marks the regime that a subdocument describes.
2054	Lookahead: 1
2055	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2056	— the regime this subdoc describes.
2057	The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
2058	{axvr_regime}
2059	in the parent document.
2060	
2061	[[axhrv_variable]]
2062	{axhrv_variable}::
2063	Marks an ungrouped regime variable in a subdocument.
2064	Lookahead: 1
2065	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2066	— the variable being detailed.
2067	
2068	[[axhrgb_group]]
2069	{axhrgb_group}::
2070	Marks a group boundary within a subdocument.
2071	Lookahead: 1
2072	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2073	— the group being detailed.
2074	The referenced attribute must match an anchor annotated with
2075	{axvr_group}
2076	in the parent document.
2077	
2078	[[axhrgc_gate]]
2079	{axhrgc_gate}::
2080	Marks a conditional activation gate within a group.
2081	Lookahead: 2
2082	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2083	— the enumerated variable (voicing
2084	{axt_enumeration})
2085	and the specific
2086	{axt_enum_value}
2087	that activates this gate.
2088	The gate variable may belong to a different regime (cross-regime gating is legal).
2089	
2090	[[axhrgv_variable]]
2091	{axhrgv_variable}::
2092	Marks a variable within the current group in a subdocument.
2093	Lookahead: 1
2094	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2095	— the variable being detailed within the current group scope.
2096

2211	== Axial Lexicon Structural Terms

2213	[[axl_definition_site]]
2214	{axl_definition_site}::

2237	{axl_definition_site_s}
2238	and

2240	paralleling the regime annotation pattern.

2251	{axo_procedure}

2283	{mcm_attribute_reference}

2286	{mcm_attribute_reference}

2298	Optional

2300	Optional

2302	(the third
2303	{mcm_attribute_reference}

2305	{axvo_group}).

2310	{axo_group}
2311	definition.

2313	The first
2314	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2315	is the group being defined.

2317	{mcm_attribute_reference}

2322	optional

2324	{axd_slash_command},

2330	from a tabtarget-style dispatch convention.

2332	{mcm_quoin};

2337	{mcm_sprue}

== Axial Format Terms

{axf_format_s}
specify how configuration data is serialized and carried.
{axrg_assignment_s}
use a particular
{axf_format}
to encode
{axrg_variable}
values in files that can be sourced, included, or parsed.

[[axf_format]]
{axf_format}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a serialization encoding for configuration data.
{axf_format_s}
determine how
{axrg_assignment_s}
are written to and read from files.

[[axf_bash]]
{axf_bash}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing bash script format for configuration.
Files are source-able via `. file` or `source file`.
Variables declared as `VAR=value` without export, or `export VAR=value` for environment propagation.

[[axf_makefile]]
{axf_makefile}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing makefile format for configuration.
Files are includable via `include file`.
Variables declared as `VAR := value` for immediate expansion or `VAR = value` for deferred expansion.

[[axf_json]]
{axf_json}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing JSON format for configuration.
Standard JSON object structure with string keys mapping to typed values.
Parsed by standard JSON libraries.

[[axf_yaml]]
{axf_yaml}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing YAML format for configuration.
Superset of JSON with additional syntax for readability.
Parsed by YAML libraries with attention to version-specific semantics.

== Axial Regime Terms

{axrg_regime_s}
are structured configuration systems that coordinate specification, validation, and rendering of typed configuration data.

[[axrg_regime]]
{axrg_regime}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a structured configuration system.
A
{axrg_regime}
binds related
{axrg_variable_s}
under a namespace prefix, with coordinated specification, validation, and rendering components.
The specification defines what can be configured;
{axrg_assignment_s}
provide specific values.

[[axrg_variable]]
{axrg_variable}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a named configuration element at schema level.
Each
{axrg_variable}
belongs to an
{axrg_regime}
and defines a name, type constraints, and relationships with other variables.
{axrg_variable_s}
are the "what can be configured" definitions;
{axrg_assignment_s}
bind values to them.

[[axrg_assignment]]
{axrg_assignment}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a specific value bound to an
{axrg_variable}.
{axrg_assignment_s}
are carried in files of a particular
{axf_format}
(bash, makefile, JSON, etc.).
Multiple assignment files may exist for the same
{axrg_regime},
each providing different configuration instances.

[[axrg_prefix]]
{axrg_prefix}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing the stable character sequence that namespaces all
{axrg_variable_s}
within an
{axrg_regime}.
Typically composed of a project marker and regime marker (e.g., `RBRR_` = `RB` + `RR` + `_`).
Once established, an
{axrg_prefix}
becomes deeply embedded across code, configuration files, and documentation.
Changing an
{axrg_prefix}
requires coordinated refactoring across all consuming artifacts.

== Axial Regime Provenance Dimensions

Provenance dimensions qualify
{axrg_assignment}
to declare how values arrive at
{axvr_kindle}
time.
These are valid dimensions on
{axvr_regime}
declaring which provenance(s) a regime supports.

[[axrd_file_sourced]]
{axrd_file_sourced}::
Assignments loaded from a persistent file via source or equivalent mechanism.
The file path is known at load time and the regime module is responsible for locating and sourcing it.

[[axrd_env_inherited]]
{axrd_env_inherited}::
Assignments received as environment variables from a parent process.
The regime module finds variables already in scope; no file sourcing occurs.

[[axrd_constructed]]
{axrd_constructed}::
Assignments computed or set programmatically before kindle.
Values are assembled by application logic rather than loaded from a static source.

== Axial Regime Annotation Terms

Regime annotations use the prefix-discriminated form (`//ax...` with no space after `//`) to provide structured metadata about regime definitions in AsciiDoc specifications.
Two complementary annotation families enable regime specification across parent documents and their subdocuments.

=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvr_*)

{axvr_*} annotations appear in parent documents between anchor and definition, declaring the regime role of the term being defined.
The surface syntax follows the prefix-discriminated pattern:

[source,asciidoc]
----
[[rbrv_sigil]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
{rbrv_sigil}::
Vessel identifier for build configurations.
----

The annotation line starts with `//axvr_` (no space after `//`), followed by the specific voicing term, then space-separated dimension and type terms.
The anchor immediately preceding the annotation must match the first
{mcm_attribute_reference}
in the definition text that follows.

[[axvr_regime]]
{axvr_regime}::
Voices a regime definition.
The annotated term defines a structured configuration system that binds related
{axrg_variable_s}
under a namespace prefix.
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
{axf_format}
(specifying how
{axrg_assignment_s}
are carried).

[[axvr_variable]]
{axvr_variable}::
Voices a regime variable definition.
The annotated term defines a named configuration element within a regime.
The definition text must include, as its second
{mcm_attribute_reference},
the parent regime (an anchor that voices
{axrg_regime}).
This ties each variable unequivocally to its regime.
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
{axd_required}
or
{axd_optional},
type
{axl_motif}
(base type or type voicing).

[[axvr_group]]
{axvr_group}::
Voices a regime group definition.
The annotated term defines a named grouping of related
{axrg_variable_s}
within a regime.
The definition text must include, as its second
{mcm_attribute_reference},
the parent regime (an anchor that voices
{axrg_regime}).
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
{axd_conditional}
(group activation depends on gate conditions in subdocument).

[[axvr_kindle]]
{axvr_kindle}::
Voices a regime kindle operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the silent initialization phase for exactly one
{axrg_regime}:
setting defaults, detecting unexpected variables, and building derived structures.

[[axvr_validate]]
{axvr_validate}::
Voices a regime validate operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the strict verification phase for exactly one
{axrg_regime}:
checking all type and constraint requirements, failing on first violation.

[[axvr_render]]
{axvr_render}::
Voices a regime render operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the diagnostic display phase for exactly one
{axrg_regime}:
producing human-readable presentation of current state.
Rendering occurs before validation so all fields are visible on failure.

[[axvr_list]]
{axvr_list}::
Voices a regime list operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the enumeration operation for exactly one
{axrd_manifold}
{axrg_regime}:
listing available instances.

[[axvr_survey]]
{axvr_survey}::
Voices a regime survey operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the read-only cross-instance summary operation for exactly one
{axrd_manifold}
{axrg_regime}.

[[axvr_audit]]
{axvr_audit}::
Voices a regime audit operation definition.
The annotated term is a dedicated
{mcm_quoin}
naming the cross-instance validation plus external verification operation for exactly one
{axrd_manifold}
{axrg_regime}.

=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhr*_)

Hierarchy markers appear in regime subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
from the following text to establish hierarchical context.

The surface syntax:

[source,asciidoc]
----
//axhrb_regime
{rbrv_regime}

//axhrgv_variable
{rbrv_sigil}
----

The hierarchy marker line starts with `//axhr` (no space after `//`), followed by the sub-prefix identifying the marker type.
Following text contains the
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
that the marker reads ahead.

==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities

[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
|===
|Marker |Lookahead |Reads

|{axhrb_regime}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The regime this subdoc describes
|{axhrv_variable}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An ungrouped regime variable
|{axhrgb_group}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A group (must match {axvr_group} in parent)
|{axhrgc_gate}      |2 {mcm_attribute_reference_s}|Enumerated variable + enum value
|{axhrgv_variable}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A variable within current group
|{axhro_kindle}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level kindle operation for this regime
|{axhro_validate}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level validate operation for this regime
|{axhro_render}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level render operation for this regime
|{axhro_list}       |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level list operation for this regime
|{axhro_survey}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level survey operation for this regime
|{axhro_audit}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level audit operation for this regime
|===

[[axhrb_regime]]
{axhrb_regime}::
Marks the regime that a subdocument describes.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the regime this subdoc describes.
The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
{axvr_regime}
in the parent document.

[[axhrv_variable]]
{axhrv_variable}::
Marks an ungrouped regime variable in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the variable being detailed.

[[axhrgb_group]]
{axhrgb_group}::
Marks a group boundary within a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the group being detailed.
The referenced attribute must match an anchor annotated with
{axvr_group}
in the parent document.

[[axhrgc_gate]]
{axhrgc_gate}::
Marks a conditional activation gate within a group.
Lookahead: 2
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
— the enumerated variable (voicing
{axt_enumeration})
and the specific
{axt_enum_value}
that activates this gate.
The gate variable may belong to a different regime (cross-regime gating is legal).

[[axhrgv_variable]]
{axhrgv_variable}::
Marks a variable within the current group in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the variable being detailed within the current group scope.

[[axhro_kindle]]
{axhro_kindle}::
Marks a regime kindle operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level kindle operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_kindle}
in the parent document).

[[axhro_validate]]
{axhro_validate}::
Marks a regime validate operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level validate operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_validate}
in the parent document).

[[axhro_render]]
{axhro_render}::
Marks a regime render operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level render operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_render}
in the parent document).

[[axhro_list]]
{axhro_list}::
Marks a regime list operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level list operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_list}
in the parent document).
Only valid on
{axrd_manifold}
regimes.

[[axhro_survey]]
{axhro_survey}::
Marks a regime survey operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level survey operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_survey}
in the parent document).
Only valid on
{axrd_manifold}
regimes.

[[axhro_audit]]
{axhro_audit}::
Marks a regime audit operation description in a subdocument.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the kit-level audit operation term (which must be voiced
{axvr_audit}
in the parent document).
Only valid on
{axrd_manifold}
regimes.

==== Nesting Rules

Hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:

* Higher-level markers implicitly close previous scope (no end markers needed)
* {axhrgb_group},
{axhrgc_gate},
and
{axhrgv_variable}
must appear within
{axhrb_regime}
scope
* {axhrgc_gate}
and
{axhrgv_variable}
must appear within
{axhrgb_group}
scope
* Multiple
{axhrgc_gate}
within one group have AND semantics
* {axhro_kindle},
{axhro_validate},
{axhro_render},
{axhro_list},
{axhro_survey},
and
{axhro_audit}
must appear within
{axhrb_regime}
scope but outside any
{axhrgb_group}
scope

==== Cross-Document Validation

* {axhrb_regime}
attribute reference must match a regime defined with
{axvr_regime}
in parent
* {axhrgb_group}
attribute reference must match a group defined with
{axvr_group}
under that regime
* {axhrgc_gate}
first attribute reference must voice
{axt_enumeration};
second must be an
{axt_enum_value}
of that specific enumeration type

== Axial Lexicon Structural Terms

[[axl_definition_site]]
{axl_definition_site}::
The location in a parent document where a linked term is defined: anchor, voicing annotation, and definition text.
The
{axl_definition_site}
establishes the term's identity, role, and architectural relationships.
Voicing annotations at the
{axl_definition_site}
may impose positional requirements on linked terms in the definition text that follows.

[[axl_detail_site]]
{axl_detail_site}::
The location where a linked term's details are elaborated through hierarchy markers and prose.
Typically a subdocument included from the parent, but may be inline within the parent document for compact definitions.
The
{axl_detail_site}
is opened by a hierarchy block marker (e.g.,
{axhob_operation},
{axhrb_regime})
and contains subordinate markers for parameters, steps, variables, or other structural elements.

== Axial Voicing Operation Terms

Two annotation families enable operation specification across
{axl_definition_site_s}
and
{axl_detail_site_s},
paralleling the regime annotation pattern.

=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvo_*)

{axvo_*} annotations appear at the
{axl_definition_site}
between anchor and definition, declaring the operation role of the term being defined.

[[axvo_procedure]]
{axvo_procedure}::
Voices an
{axo_procedure}
definition.
The annotated term defines an executable operation with defined inputs, behavior, outputs, and completion semantics.
No entity affiliation required.
The first
{mcm_attribute_reference}
is the procedure being defined.
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
exactly one lifecycle
({axd_transient},
{axd_longrunning},
or
{axd_periodic}).
Optional
{axd_attended}.
Optional
{axd_internal}.
Optional
{axd_grouped}
(the second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
must be a term voiced
{axvo_group}).

[[axvo_method]]
{axvo_method}::
Voices an
{axo_method}
definition.
The annotated term defines an executable operation belonging to an entity's interface.
The first
{mcm_attribute_reference}
is the method being defined.
The second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
must be the affiliated entity (a term voiced
{axo_entity}).
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
exactly one lifecycle
({axd_transient},
{axd_longrunning},
or
{axd_periodic}).
Optional
{axd_attended}.
Optional
{axd_internal}.
Optional
{axd_grouped}
(the third
{mcm_attribute_reference}
must be a term voiced
{axvo_group}).

[[axvo_group]]
{axvo_group}::
Voices an
{axo_group}
definition.
The annotated term defines a named collection of related operations that share an affiliated entity.
The first
{mcm_attribute_reference}
is the group being defined.
The second
{mcm_attribute_reference}
must be the affiliated entity (a term voiced
{axo_entity}).
+
{axd_dimension_s}:
optional
{axd_tabtarget},
{axd_slash_command},
or other exposure mechanism specifying the default interface for external members.

[[axvo_tabtarget]]
{axvo_tabtarget}::
Voices a tabtarget — one concrete CLI invocation routed via filename
from a tabtarget-style dispatch convention.
Each tabtarget receives exactly one
{mcm_quoin};
per-platform or per-target variants are catalogued separately rather
than collapsed under a shared sprue list.
+
1-arity: reads one
{mcm_sprue}
from following text — the literal routing identifier of the concrete
CLI invocation as it appears on disk.
The
{mcm_sprue}
is the exact wire-name; the
{mcm_quoin}
is the concrete CLI invocation concept.
Sprue-lookahead mechanism mirrors
{axr_member}.
+
Qualified by
{axd_dimension_s}
in
{axl_annotation}.
The
{axd_imprint}
dimension types a filename-embedded folio slot when present;
absence declares the tabtarget has no embedded folio.
+
Operation detail sites may attach to
{axvo_tabtarget}
quoins via
{axhob_operation},
reusing the procedure/method hierarchy markers without alteration.
Second-order constraints — which markers apply, which don't,
under what conditions — are deferred to future articulation.

=== New Operation Dimension Terms

[[axd_attended]]
{axd_attended}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that a procedure or method requires human interaction during execution.
Absence means unattended (fully automated).
Valid on
{axvo_procedure}
and
{axvo_method}.

[[axd_internal]]
{axd_internal}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that a procedure or method is an implementation building block, not a consumer-facing interface.
Absence means external (published interface).
Valid on
{axvo_procedure}
and
{axvo_method}.

[[axd_grouped]]
{axd_grouped}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that a procedure or method belongs to a named operation group.
Imposes a positional requirement on the definition text: the next
{mcm_attribute_reference}
after any prior positional requirements must be an
{axo_group}.
For
{axvo_procedure}:
the second linked term must be the group.
For
{axvo_method}:
the third linked term must be the group (second is the entity).
Valid on
{axvo_procedure}
and
{axvo_method}.

[[axd_tabtarget]]
{axd_tabtarget}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that external members of an operation group are exposed as tabtargets.
Valid on
{axvo_group}.

[[axd_slash_command]]
{axd_slash_command}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that external members of an operation group are exposed as
{axi_cc_slash_command_s}.
Valid on
{axvo_group}.

[[axd_imprint]]
{axd_imprint}::
A
{axd_dimension}
indicating that a tabtarget's filename embeds a folio — a parameter
value carried by the tabtarget's wire-name rather than passed as a
runtime argument.
1-arity: reads one
{mcm_attribute_reference}
from following text — the type anchor of whatever the imprint slot
names.
Must resolve to an existing type quoin; zero new terms minted.
Valid on
{axvo_tabtarget}.
+

content.text:
Yes — these should carry voicings, and there's a clear precedent in this very file dictating which one.

## Recommendation: `//axl_voices axtu_path` on all five

Every other filesystem-path quoin in RBS0 is voiced. The matching cases:

- `rbst_path` (line 3685): `//axl_voices axtu_path` — "Repository-context path, typically constructed from regime variables." This is the closest sibling to your new quoins.
- `rbrr_secrets_dir` (line 2780): `//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path`
- `rbrr_*_rbra_file` (lines 2786–2821): `//axl_voices axrd_constructed axtu_path`

`axtu_path` is defined as "a file system path... may be absolute or relative depending on context" — it covers both your directory quoins (`rbsld_*`) and file quoins (`rbsrf_*`); AXLA has no separate directory motif. So:

```
[[rbsld_moorings]]
//axl_voices axtu_path
{rbsld_moorings}::
...
```

…and likewise for `rbsld_vessels`, `rbsrf_rbrr`, `rbsrf_rbrd`, `rbsrf_rbrm`.

## What I'd deliberately *not* add

- **`axrg_variable`** — that dimension is for RBRR/RBRD schema variables (the `RBRR_*` config namespace). Your `rbsld_`/`rbsrf_` quoins are spec-level path *constants* composed in the mapping section, not regime variables. The prefix legend you wrote ("Spec Layout Directory / Spec Regime File") confirms they live outside the regime schema. Hence the bare `axtu_path`, like `rbst_path`, not the `axrg_variable axtu_path` form.

- **`axrd_constructed`** and the other `axrd_*` provenance dimensions — those qualify regime *assignments* by how their values arrive at kindle time. These quoins aren't regime fields, so provenance doesn't apply. (Note `rbrr_*_rbra_file` carries `axrd_constructed` because it's a derived RBRR field — different category from yours.)

- **`axrg_assignment`** for the `rbsrf_*` file quoins — tempting, since they name regime assignment *files*. But `axrg_assignment` is the *value bound to a variable*, not the file's location; the quoin's identity here is "the path of the file." There's no AXLA motif for "assignment-carrier file as a path" beyond `axtu_path`, and no precedent voicing `axrg_assignment` anywhere in RBS0. Keep it to the single primary motif and let the prose ("The {rbrr_regime} assignment file") carry the assignment-carrier role.

## One thing worth flagging to the parallel chat

The section preamble says a layout change "touches only the mapping section" — but `rbsld_vessels`'s definition prose hardcodes `rbmv_vessels` (`{rbsld_vessels}{rbrv_sigil}/rbrv.env`) and `rbsrf_*` prose hardcodes `rbrr.env`/`rbrd.env`/`rbrm.env`/`rbrn.env`. Those literals already live in the mapping-section replacement text, so the prose is fine as written — just confirm the lint scheme is happy with `axtu_path` quoins whose value is composed from `{rbmm_moorings_dir}` rather than a single anchor (the precedent quoins resolve to literal-ish paths, yours interpolate an attribute — should be fine, but it's the one novel wrinkle).

Net: add `//axl_voices axtu_path` to all five; nothing else.

content.text:
Short answer: AXLA has vocabulary for regime *carriage*, but no dedicated "regime assignment file path" motif — and the carriage fact is already voiced elsewhere, not on these quoins. Let me lay out what actually exists.

## What AXLA gives you for regime files

Three relevant terms, none of which is a "the file at this path" motif:

1. **`axrg_assignment`** — "a specific value bound to an `axrg_variable`... `axrg_assignment_s` are carried in files of a particular `axf_format`." Note the arity: the motif is *one value↔variable binding*. An `rbrr.env` carries the *whole regime's* bindings (many assignments), so the file is a *carrier of* `axrg_assignment_s`, not itself "an assignment." Voicing `rbsrf_rbrr` as `axrg_assignment` is a category slip — singular binding vs. multi-binding carrier.

2. **`axf_bash`** — the format dimension ("files source-able via `. file`").

3. **`axrd_file_sourced`** — the provenance dimension: "assignments loaded from a persistent file via source... the regime module is responsible for locating and sourcing it."

There's also no precedent: `grep` for `axl_voices axrg_assignment` across all RB and JJ specs returns nothing. Nobody has ever voiced an assignment-carrier file.

## The carriage fact is already voiced — on the regime quoin

Look at line 2773, the `rbrr_regime` definition site:

```
[[rbrr_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
```

`axf_bash` + `axrd_file_sourced` already says "RBRR is a bash-format regime sourced from a file on disk." The "it lives in a file" semantics belong to the regime, and that's where they're declared. The new `rbsrf_*` quoins don't re-assert that fact — they exist to give the *canonical path* a linked-term identity so prose stops repeating `rbmm_moorings/rbrr.env`. That's path indirection, full stop.

## So the voicing answer stands, but here's the honest tension

- `rbsld_moorings`, `rbsld_vessels` — directories, not carriers → `//axl_voices axtu_path`, unambiguous.
- `rbsrf_rbrr`, `rbsrf_rbrd`, `rbsrf_rbrm` — two defensible readings:
  - **`//axl_voices axtu_path`** (my recommendation): the quoin's identity is "the path." The regime affiliation rides on the prose linked term `{rbrr_regime}`, and the carriage fact already lives on `rbrr_regime` via `axrd_file_sourced`. Consistent with `rbst_path` and the other path quoins; no category slip.
  - **`//axl_voices axrg_assignment axf_bash`** if your team reads "assignment file" as a first-class carrier concept worth voicing at the path site. This is the reading your question is reaching for — and it's not crazy — but it fights the motif's own arity (one binding, not a file of them) and has zero precedent.

My call: `axtu_path` on all five. If the parallel chat *wants* to mark the assignment-carrier role at the path site, the least-wrong way is `axrg_assignment axf_bash` on the `rbsrf_*` trio only — but I'd resist it, because it duplicates carriage semantics already declared on `rbrr_regime` and stretches `axrg_assignment` past its definition. If the gap genuinely bothers the team, the clean fix is a *new* dimension/motif minted in AXLA for "assignment-carrier file," not bending `axrg_assignment`.

Worth a direct question to whoever owns AXLA: was a "regime file" motif ever intended, or is `axrd_file_sourced`-on-the-regime the deliberate single home for that fact? That settles it.

content.text:
Here's what I think is actually going on, now that the wording's in play rather than fixed.

## The stale wording is hiding a conflated concept

Read `axrg_assignment`'s own definition against itself:

> "A specific value bound to an `axrg_variable`. … `axrg_assignment_s` are carried in files of a particular `axf_format`. **Multiple assignment files may exist for the same regime, each providing different configuration instances.**"

The first sentence is one binding (`VAR=value`). The last sentence is about *files* and *configuration instances* — a different, coarser concept. The motif is straddling two layers:

1. **the binding** — one variable↔value pair
2. **the carrier** — the artifact holding *one complete instance's* assignment set (a `.env` file, an inherited env scope, a constructed struct)

A `rbrr.env` isn't "an assignment" — it's a carrier of many. That straddle is exactly why `rbsrf_*` had no clean voicing: the layer it belongs to (carrier) doesn't have its own motif.

## The clean fix is idiomatic AXLA, and you already have most of it

Mint a carrier motif and let the **provenance dimension select its concrete form** — which is precisely the pattern AXLA already uses for `axo_procedure` (lifecycle via `axd_transient/longrunning/periodic`), `axt_enum_value` (encoding via `axd_string`), and `axi_protocol` (transport via `axd_file_bus`). So:

- **`axrg_assignment`** — narrow it back to *one binding*. Drop the "carried in files / configuration instances" clause.
- **`axrg_carrier`** (new) — the artifact holding one instance's complete assignment set. Its concrete form is given by the `axrd_*` provenance already in the lexicon:
  - `axrd_file_sourced` carrier ⟹ a file at a path (format via `axf_*`)
  - `axrd_env_inherited` carrier ⟹ a process environment scope
  - `axrd_constructed` carrier ⟹ an in-memory struct

The consistency check that tells me this is the right cut: **`axrd_singleton`/`axrd_manifold` already count carriers.** A singleton regime has exactly one carrier; a manifold regime has one per instance. Those dimensions have been quietly describing carrier cardinality all along — they were waiting for the noun.

## How the quoins then voice

- `rbsld_moorings`, `rbsld_vessels` → `//axl_voices axtu_path`. They're *containers*, not carriers — directories that hold carriers. Plain path.
- `rbsrf_rbrr`, `rbsrf_rbrd`, `rbsrf_rbrm` → `//axl_voices axrg_carrier axrd_file_sourced`. The replacement text being a path is now *correct by definition*: a file-sourced carrier's locus is its path. Format (`axf_bash`) I'd **inherit** from the `{rbrr_regime}` link in prose rather than restate — `rbrr_regime` already declares `axf_bash axrd_file_sourced`, and restating it at the carrier site is non-load-bearing duplication.

This also retroactively makes `rbrr_regime`'s existing `//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton` read better: the regime declares *that* it's carried as a singleton file-sourced bash carrier; the `rbsrf_rbrr` quoin names *where that carrier lives*.

## The pivot question — is it worth minting?

Mint `axrg_carrier` only if you want the **type-scheme to reason about carriers**. The payoff is real cross-validation:

- every `axrd_file_sourced` regime *must* have a carrier quoin with a declared path
- manifold-regime carrier paths *must* be templated (vary per instance), singleton paths *must* be literal — which is exactly the `rbsld_vessels{rbrv_sigil}/rbrv.env` vs `rbmm_moorings/rbrr.env` distinction your new section is drawing by hand

If instead path-indirection is just a *presentation* convenience ("stop repeating the literal"), then `axtu_path` on all five is honest and you only need to **freshen `axrg_assignment`'s wording** to stop it overreaching into files — no new motif, no carrier validation.

My lean: you're already hand-drawing the singleton-vs-manifold path distinction in the new section prose, which means the carrier concept is *latent in the spec whether or not you name it*. That's usually the signal to mint — the concept is load-bearing, it just doesn't have a home yet. I'd mint `axrg_carrier`.

Two things I'd want your call on before drafting AXLA edits:

1. **Name** — `axrg_carrier` (physical artifact framing) vs `axrg_instance` (logical "one configuration instance" framing). They're 1:1, so it's a readability pick. At the use site the prose says "the RBRR regime *file*," which is physical → I lean `axrg_carrier`.
2. **Scope** — narrow `axrg_assignment` and mint the carrier as one coherent edit, or stage it (mint carrier now, leave `axrg_assignment`'s stale clause for a later pass)?

Want me to draft the AXLA mapping-section entries and definition blocks for `axrg_carrier` + the trimmed `axrg_assignment`, plus the five voicing lines in RBS0?

content.text:
Confirmed in the codebase — and it sharpens the model nicely.

**The virtual regimes are real and they're carriers-without-files.** RBGC self-describes as "Tinder constants (pure string literals… available at source time)" and RBDC as "Derived Constants" computed at kindle from other regimes. Their "carrier" isn't a `.env` — it's the bash module that *defines* the variables and gets sourced into consumers. That's exactly your "defined in one place, passed to other components." In provenance terms they're `axrd_constructed` (RBDC derives, RBGC assembles literals), not `axrd_file_sourced`.

Meanwhile every regime that *is* documented with `axvr_regime` (RBRR/RBRD/RBRM singleton, RBRV/RBRN manifold) is `axrd_file_sourced`. So the spec only ever voiced the file-backed ones — which is precisely why the carrier concept never had to exist until now, and why the wording went stale unnoticed.

## What this forces in the `axrg_carrier` definition

The carrier must **never presume a file** — its concrete form is provenance-polymorphic:

- `axrd_file_sourced` → a file at a path (encoding via `axf_*`)
- `axrd_constructed` → values assembled by a defining module, sourced into consumers *(RBDC, RBGC)*
- `axrd_env_inherited` → a process environment scope

And a clean validation rule falls straight out of your observation:

> **A carrier has a path locus iff its provenance is `axrd_file_sourced`.** Virtual carriers (`axrd_constructed`, `axrd_env_inherited`) have no consumer-facing path.

That's the rule that makes minting worth it — the type-scheme can now reject anyone trying to give RBDC an `rbsrf_`-style path, and conversely flag a file-sourced regime missing its carrier path.

One more wrinkle the codebase imposes: **manifold file-sourced carriers stay templated, not quoined.** Your mapping-section comment (lines 345–346) already decided RBRV/RBRN paths are composed in prose (`{rbsld_vessels}{rbrv_sigil}/rbrv.env`) with no monolithic quoin. So the carrier motif resolves to a single named quoin only for *singleton* file-sourced regimes (the `rbsrf_*` trio); manifold ones are voiced in prose. The carrier definition should say that explicitly.

## Proposed wording (for your reaction)

`axrg_carrier` definition:

```
[[axrg_carrier]]
{axrg_carrier}::
A {axl_motif} representing the artifact holding one instance's complete set
of {axrg_assignment_s} for a single {axrg_regime}. The carrier's concrete
form is selected by its provenance {axd_dimension}: {axrd_file_sourced}
manifests as a file at a path (encoding via {axf_format}); {axrd_constructed}
as values assembled by a defining module and sourced into consumers;
{axrd_env_inherited} as an inherited process environment scope. A
{axrd_singleton} regime has exactly one carrier; a {axrd_manifold} regime has
one carrier per instance. Only a {axrd_file_sourced} carrier has a path locus
— a singleton's locus is named by one quoin whose replacement text is that
path; a manifold's loci are templated and composed in prose. Virtual carriers
({axrd_constructed}, {axrd_env_inherited}) have no consumer-facing path.
```

Trim `axrg_assignment` back to just the binding (delete the "carried in files / configuration instances" clause — that work now belongs to `axrg_carrier`).

Then in RBS0: `rbsld_*` → `//axl_voices axtu_path` (containers, not carriers); `rbsrf_rbrr/rbrd/rbrm` → `//axl_voices axrg_carrier axrd_file_sourced`.

## Two scope calls before I draft the edits

1. **The `axrd_*` preamble** in AXLA currently says provenance dimensions "qualify `axrg_assignment`… valid on `axvr_regime`." Under the new model they qualify the **carrier**. Want me to reword that preamble too, or leave it and just add the carrier?
2. **Do you want the file-sourced⟺path rule written as enforceable type-scheme language** (so the linter can act on it), or just stated as prose guidance in the carrier definition for now?

Tell me on those two and I'll draft the full set — AXLA mapping entries + `axrg_carrier` definition + trimmed `axrg_assignment` (+ reworded preamble if you want it), and the five voicing lines in RBS0.

lastPrompt: I do prefer carrier.  And yes, check our codebase: not every regime has an actual file; some are 'virtual', i.e. a set of variables defined in one place then passed to other components.
